review: The skirt is very full and wonderfully, wonderfully heavy.  It really is a ton of skirt for the price and it's very well made.  If you bought this anywhere else, it would be at *least* fifty bucks.  If this was currently in style, stores like Banana Republic would charge hundreds of dollars and you'd kind of understand why:  it's just gorgeously made.The only issue I have is that I bought this to wear on a regular basis - I didn't want a costume skirt.  And was it not for the color (navy blue), I could've pulled that off.  Unfortunately it's strangely shiny.  If you're aiming to belly dance in this thing, the gauzy material would be totally sexy and mysterious in dim light, but in daylight it looks like the bottom of a fairly classy 80s prom dress - so it's better than what you'd find at a typical prom in the 80s, but it's still something you'd find almost exclusively in the 80s.Definitely a good buy if you're wanting to play dress up, but not something you'd wear casually.

There's no question the boots are well made, and it's nice to have that recognized brand on the back of the boot, but I'm having trouble justifying the additional $$ for the brand. I ordered a size up to accommodate for the fluffy fit in the boot, and that was a good choice. I'm finding the upper part of the boot fits a little more snug that previous boots I've purchased. It's not too small, just tighter, so be aware of that, especially if you don't have pencil-thin legs. These are very nice boots, definitely far above the bargain options you'll find at your local big box stores, but they are quite pricey compared to some good quality alternatives that are mid-priced. Love the boot, don't love the price.

review: I'm actually used to larger watches, but this one looks very nice even though it's a little smaller than I thought it would be.  Had to remove 2 pieces from the band and now it fits perfectly.  I am having a little trouble getting the date set - I can't seem to find the right spot to have the date change at midnight instead of noon - I've tried resetting it a couple of times (the date setting is a little touchy to find but it's there, just pull the knob out 1/2 way instead of all the way), and I've set the watch back 1/2 a day manually as well (though adjustment of the hands not the date) but neither as worked.  Hopefully I'll get that part working soon enough.I really like that it's an Eco-Drive, and that it's black. I wear my watch sun up to bed time and after only a couple of weeks, though, and I can see a couple spots on the band that are wearing through the black color already.  If that continues, I'll have to lower my review.  I do type a lot at work, but that piece is normally actually resting on a cloth-covered gel wrist pad.  I may be forced to not wear this around the house, just to try to keep the finish from wearing off.  I'll update this review once I've had it a bit longer to let you know about the color.Update 9/10/13:  The watch itself is great, holding up well - but as I feared the parts on the underside of the watch that will touch a table/desk/keyboard rest when my hand is palm down is fading more and more as time goes on.  It's not TERRIBLY noticeable unless you're looking straight at it, but not only are the edges a little worn down to the silver underneath but there is also a chip in the middle of the band as well.  Great watch, just wish their coating was more durable.-D

review: I ordered this wallet to reduce the bulge created by having lots of credit cards in the standard wallet, and to be able to find each of my cards when I open my wallet. After searching the internet, I concluded the price asked for this one was very reasonable by comparison.The quality of the leather and the construction is fine.  The leather is smooth and soft, and the stitching is even.  There's a plastic window for one card (like an ID), and a plastic insert if you have even more cards, although I think if you filled it, you'd have a pretty bulky item.Yes, you can get better quality if you don't mind paying about four times as much, otherwise, I don't think you will. It should last a couple of years.  I find the complaints about the tightness of the card slots a exaggerated.  I had no trouble fitting a dozen different plastic cards into the slots; with paper cards that easily crumple, it's a little more difficult. Yes, it can be a little difficult to slide out cards sometimes, but not remarkably so.  I'm happy with the purchase.

review: I got this as a replacement to my more traditional type leather wallet. I have never had one of these metal clamshell type wallets before and here is my impression after a week of use. The wallet does look pretty sleek and is a departure from any type of "traditional" wallet. It sort of reminds me of the old cigarette cases you see the people using in older movies. It makes me want to hold it up to someone and say "Want a smoke?" even though I don't smoke. :) When it is open it is very easy to access everything in it since the interior fans out. It's also really light. When I have it in my back pocket I don't really even notice and even when setting down it's surprisingly comfortable. It supposedly will protect your cards from RFID readers supposedly, but I have not tested this myself.There are a few downsides though. To put cash in it you have to fold the bills into at least thirds or smaller. It's sort of annoying to have to refold money every time you get some new bills. Also, if you have very many bills it won't let the wallet close. For me I can only have about five bills max. This number will probably vary depending upon how many cards you have in it (I have about six cards in mine). After a week of normal use it already has a few small dents in it. I have not dropped it and this is just from normal wear so I was a little disappointed with that. The dents are only cosmetic, though, and haven't affected the functionality of the wallet. I keep my wallet in my back pocket so on one level that isn't surprising, but on the other hand since this is a very common place to keep a wallet I would expect it should be able to go longer than a week before it starts getting dented.Overall, if you don't carry a lot of bills and cards I think you might like this wallet. For me, I just rated it three stars. One off for having to fold the cash so small and another off for the denting. Of course if you keep your wallet in other places than your back pocket the denting might not be an issue for you either.
summary: Decent wallet if you don't carry much cash

review: I highly recommend these boots.  I think they look great.  They are very comfortable and warm. I have perhaps too many boots, but I will compare them to help you pick the right boot for you.  The boots I will review are BearPaws, Emu, Uggs, and Minnetonka. All the boots I am reviewing here are fully lined with sheep fleece, including a fleece lined foot bed.  They will all keep your feet warm and comfortable.  If you have boots that are lined with synthetic fleece they will not keep your feet comfortable.  Your feet will not be as warm as with wool and ironically will tend to sweat, which is uncomfortable and will eventually chill your feet.  The two best quality boots of these are the Emus and the Uggs.BearPaws are the bargain boots here, in a good way.  BearPaws make warm and comfortable boots that are good looking.  The boot interior is wool so they do keep your feet warm.  The exterior leather is not sheepskin, but is cow hide.  The cowhide makes for a sturdy boot.  These are the boots to wear walking in snow.  Cowhide is stiffer and I think a bit tougher than the sheepskin, though it is not as supple as the sheepskin.  Bear Paws are not as well made with the attention to detail that you find with Emu.  However, cost about a third what Uggs and Emus cost and are well made.  They look good, are comfortable and warm.  Considering the quality and the price, you can't go wrong with these boots!Emu boots are a sturdiest of these boots.  The edges are finished and bound rather than just a raw cut like the Uggs.  They have a little bit of arch support in them.  The wool they use is 100% Australian Merino wool and is densest of all the boots.  It tends not to compress over time and will insulate your feet well.  You may need to order a size up because of this.  The sole has a tougher outer layer of rubber so they tend to wear well.  I think these are better made than the wildly popular Uggs Australia boots.  They are fairly water repellent, even before being treated, probably because of a high natural lanolin content.  These are great boots to walk in the city in on a winter day.Uggs Australia boots are the softest of my boots. The Uggs have a flat sole without much arch support, but the wool foot bed does support your foot fairly well.  They are like wearing slippers.  The wool is soft and the sheepskin exterior is supple.  They say their wool is treated and it feels like it is combed or something to be silkier.  More than any other of my boots the Uggs need to be treated with water repellent, though all the boots here should be sprayed to help protect them and keep them clean.  Uggs makes their own spray and it works well.  Any good brand made for suede and nubuck works well.  Avoid using any of the water proof sprays with silicone as an ingredient though.  Uggs are the most comfortable boots for lounging around in a cabin.Minnetonka makes a very soft and comfy boot too.  These American made boots come in materials other than sheepskin.  I'm only reviewing the sheepskin side zip fringe boot, because that is the only one I know about.  It is all sheepskin and soft like a slipper.  It has a rubber sole and cushioned insole.  These are great to wear in the house or on a car trip, but I doubt they would hold up well to much outdoors walking.  I really love these boots for wearing at home.  They keep my feet warm and comfy.
summary: Great price, decent quality in a cute warm and comfortable boot

review: First of all, I am not going to whine and complain about the belt on this product being waaaaay too long for my perfectly sculpted and amazingly buff little body.  You're welcome.  (The belt length and item dimensions are right there in the product description, and it IS described as being a one-size-fits-many product.)I wanted to try out this money belt because I am going to be travelling to several different countries in the upcoming year, and it seems only prudent to have some place to stash important items such as a passport or credit cards.  I did not want a neck pouch or wallet, because I know someone who had the strap on her neck wallet pulled tight by a thief, and twisted, like a garrotte.  Ah, no thanks.  (And that happened in a "perfectly safe" city in the USA, by the way.)I tested the money belt by wearing it all day for a few days, with my passport tucked inside.  I was completely comfortable with it on, actually to the point of not being particularly aware of it.  This being February, however, I did not have the opportunity to test it out during warm weather.The only reason that I did not give it five stars is because I have no idea how durable it will prove to be. It seems to be quite adequately constructed, however, and lightweight zippers are appropriate for the product.For those concerned that items inside might get damp from perspiration in warm weather, it might help to wrap your passport or cash in a plastic sandwich bag prior to putting it in the money belt.I'm very pleased with this product, and am sure that it will be quite useful when travelling.  This also might be a good product to wear when attending football games and such, to avoid dragging along a bulky wallet or handbag.
summary: Seems perfect for the job intended

review: I'm technically a US7.5 B(M), but generally go up half a size for pointy-toed shoes. That's what I did with this pair, and they are on the loose side of comfortable. They are remarkable because they don't squish the toes, and are as roomy as round-toed shoes. There is enough leather at the sides and back to contain the whole foot comfortably, and this gives a good, secure feeling. I saw no need to put in any padding or heel grips; in fact, I just came back from lunchtime grocery shopping in them and my feet feel just fine, with no blisters on heels or bunions. The entire insole is padded and the outsole is decently grippy. Nine West clearly know what they're doing here. It's lovely to find a pair of work shoes that are sensible, professional and versatile all at the same time.

review: Believe me.. I've tried many of Patty's tops and dresses.  This one just WORKS for everybody.  The only person that may not like as well is someone 5 ft 1" or under since the torso might be too long.  This hides a myriad of sins, is warm enough for colder months, fine for summer in air conditioning and can be worn casually or dressy.  Don't be turned off by the big buttons (I was at first)..  Don't be.  They look just right on this top.  It will flatter your chest and shoulders with the perfect neckline.  It's soft so the sleeves aren't tight, but they're long.  Long and soft enough to push up if you want.  Thick in the middle?  No worries. The way this is made it will camouflage that extra weight many of us baby boomer women carry in our midsection.The top is sexy, but in a conservative way if that makes sense. This one isn't too low cut on me, while it would show "enough" cleavage for bigger busted gals.  You can pull the cowl up and fold it over a little if you find it too low - and it stays!  The fabrication is perfect making it a perfect transitional piece.  You might considering ordering down if you're between sizes.  This top has enough give and stretch, but the material isn't a bit sleazy.  Patty has some tops which are more questionable in terms of fabrication and being as well made as this one!!!I can find absolutely nothing wrong with this beautiful PattyB.  I just want women to know THEY DON'T NEED TO WORRY ONE LITTLE BIT when ordering this one. I know I sweat ordering some of Patty's tops- simply not sure about the quality and fit.  This top I'll soon have ordered in every color available.  This is certainly one that IS worth the price.  I can't say that about all PB tops.Now.. here's my original review.  I just wanted women to know this is truly a no-brainer.  You'll love it and want to keep it- and possibly order more for your sisters and girl friends. It has a consistent fit in all colors.______________________________________________________________This one in size small fits perfectly.  I'm 5' 6" tall, around 120 lbs and long torso.  This top is cute and fits like a dream.  I started with the chocolate, came back for gray (haven't received) and lastly ordered in black (just received but haven't tried on and praying it fits like the brown).Don't hesitate on this adorable top.  It looks just like the photo on the bod.  It's my fav and soon I'll own 3 if the other 2 fit like the chocolate.  You'll LOVE it!

review: I've got a bunch of stuff from Columbia, not only is every item of superior quality, but reasonably-priced as well.  Seriously, when it comes to sportswear, and particularly items like jackets and such, the mark-ups are often ludicrously unjustified.  One particularly severe example of this phenomenon I experienced involved a soft-shell jacket from one of the upmarket manufacturers.  The MSRP of that jacket, which protected against neither wind nor rain, was $160.Columbia doesn't pull shenanigans like that, which is why I sought out this brand for an cheap windbreaker, and as the review title indicates, I do love it.  It's got a decently roomy cut for layering but isn't all puffy, it has a proper anti-precipitation coating that actually works, and it has the all-important vent in the back to keep the wearer from overheating (crucial for cycling,) and the cell phone pocket up front.  Put a polyester fleece sweatshirt or wool sweater on underneath and you're ready for anything, provided due attention is given to pants and headwear.  And it looks cool even though it's cheap.Additionally, a note about Columbia's customer service:  Not long ago, I had a zipper go bad on a Columbia jacket I had owned for about two years.  I sent the jacket back with an explanation of my plight.  Shortly thereafter, they sent me a replacement.  I also learned that they give their returned and/or damaged clothing away to charity to be repaired and distributed to the needy.  That is very cool.

review: I have never worn slippers before.  Always just went barefoot.  As I get older and we are doing some construction on the house I have found slippers necessary.  I wanted something with a closed toe so that if I bump into lumber or whatever from the construction that is around my toes would be somewhat protected.  I have found I also need some arch support (no correction, just support) as I get older.I ended up putting SuperFeet insoles in these to get the perfect arch support but I have really high arches so I think the insoles that came with these would be fine for most people.  The insoles that came with these have some arch support built in - enough that at first I thought I could just use those but ultimately decided to switch the insoles.  The original insoles are removable so this was an easy task.I have never had real wool lined slippers before either.  My feet are always hot and I thought that would be a bad thing but I was planning on wearing these without sox and it's cold here in the winter so I thought I'd give it a try. This was the nicest surprise!!!  I had heard that real wool regulates the heat naturally but I didn't believe that.  However, it does.  My feet are super comfortable in these and they don't sweat or smell bad (which is a problem with some of the other slippers I've tried).These have a bit of a lip behind the heel so your feet are secure but it's not like an ankle boot.  Soles are tough enough to wear out to the mailbox.  I have even caught myself pondering whether I REALLY had to change shoes to run to the store (I did).LOVE these!

review: I was excited to find these.  They were priced right and they hit that sweet spot between an oxford and a tennis shoe that make them nice enough to wear at a (business casual) office, but not so dressy that I can't walk to work or wear them on the weekends.  The chocolate nubuck ones also exactly matched the color of the shoes I was replacing - brown, but a dusty looking sort of brown that goes with lots.  And all those positive reviews!  Of course I bought them.When I first put them on, they weren't exactly a delight on my feet, but they weren't uncomfortable either.  The soles are thick enough to provide some protection but not so thick that I can't feel what I'm walking on.  They weren't as springy as some walking shoes I've owned, but they offered at least a little bounce.  They weren't as airy as some shoes, but I didn't overheat in the first five minutes either.  (I guess the true test will come next summer, huh?)  They seem to be well-made, and like I said, they're attractive shoes with just the right level of casualness.So "good enough," I thought, and I wore them on my walk to work.  I was satisfied for a while...and then the pain crept up.  Not pain in my feet, though.  Or even in my back.  No, they gave me shin splints.  Both legs.  My feet aren't deformed and I'm not overweight.  I have normal arches and have never needed orthopedic shoes or insoles or anything before, so I almost didn't believe they were the shoes despite that being the obvious conclusion to draw.  All I can figure is that there's something subtle in them that makes me walk just a little...off. review: My foot is 9 - 91/4" long depending on how long I've been on my feet.  They measure 3 1/2 - 3 5/8" across.  This chart didn't show it, but that's supposed to be a C width.Per the chart, a 91/8 foot should fit a 6.5 shoe.  All my shoes that fit (but too narrow) are 6.5, so what did I order?  A 6.5.C and D widths have been too tight, so I ordered an E.(And what's the deal with C/D widths?  Each has its own measurement, so don't combine them!.  It's like size 10/12 -- we used to have 9,10, 11 and 12, then we went to 9/10 and 11/12, and now it's 9101112 -- FOUR sizes in one!  But I digress...)Anyway, the width is finally perfect, but the shoe is way too big!  I am talking 1/2" too big -- I measured it.  With extra thick pillow inserts and one of those heal pads you glue to the back of the shoe, I got it down to 1/4", but I still fall out of them.  I had to wear them right away, otherwise I would have sent them back.They also hurt!  The left one rubbed against that big toe knuckle bone where people get bunions so much that it's still red 2 days later!  This is what I did with them:  walked from my house to the car, sat in the car, walked from the car (feet, not miles) into church, stood for ten minutes, sat 2 hours, stood for ten minutes, car, sit, home.  I can only imagine if I had to walk or stand any tremendous amount that night.  I would have had National No Shoes day then instead of today.  I have had NO luck buying shoes the past two years or so.  I am beginning to see why some people wear flip flops all year.  They're not nuts, they have just given up on shoe shoppin'.

review: The 2013 XS Classic Messenger is a straightforward bag with simple appointments: ballistic nylon exterior fabric, a divider (slash pocket), interior and under flap pockets, key tether, comfy 2" wide strap and quick adjust CAM buckle. There is no handle for a quick grab  'n go or cross strap for additional stability while biking or running. Although the XS Snoop insert is made for the XS Classic, fit is poor: the Classic is too small and the insert is scrunched and distorted, making it a PITA to pull cameras in and out. I also own a XSTimbuk2 Snoop Camera Messenger Bag, supposedly based on the Classic design, but it's deeper so the insert fits perfectly.Sadly, I send the XS Classic back to Amazonland. I was tempted to keep it since it's so cute and well made, but it's too small for my messenger bag duties. I need the next size up. However, if your carry needs are modest--stow an iPad, windbreaker and water bottle--this just may be the perfect mini messenger.

review: These shoes are great! Nice quality, great comfort, and a fashionable style. Much better, in my opinion, when compared to other brands, such as Sanuks, which are of low quality and are not worth the cost. While these are on the expensive side, based on a pair of Olukai flip flops I own, I have no doubt these will last long enough to show a nice ROI. I can wear these all day, walking around, and not feel the need to kick them off as soon as I get home. They aren't too warm either, being made of a fine mesh material. Even traction on wet surfaces is good, so if you wanted these as a pool/beach shoe, they'll fit the bill. Just be forewarned that, as with other Olukai shoes, the arch support is high, so if you have low arches or flat feet, these probably won't be that comfortable for you.I know most reviews say to order one size down, but I have an add-on to the advice. I have a wide foot, and went that route (ordered a 10 instead of my typical 10.5/11). But, I found that doing that left me with a shoe that BARELY fit, to the point that my toes were right up against the tip of the shoe and I had no room on the sides. I returned that pair and ordered a 10.5, which fit much better. It is slightly roomy front to back, but not overly so. With that said, I'd agree that you should probably order a size down if you have a narrow or average-width foot. If you have a wide foot, I'd suggest ordering on the lower end of your sizing if you float between sizes.
summary: Superior Summer Shoe To Other Brands

review: First of all, the lapis part of the necklace, earrings and bracelet, the color is a very dark blue, like a dark navy color.  The turquoise part of the bracelet, necklace and earrings are definitely turquoise colored, but with veining in a brownish color.Since this set is supposed to be sterling silver, I thought the color of the links would be silver, but in fact they are gold in color, but there is a tag attached to both the necklace and bracelet showing the stamp of 925, which is sterling silver.I don't normally wear bracelets because they are too hard to get on my wrist.  This bracelet was no exception. Along with the lobster clasp which this bracelet and necklace have, there are several links so that you can adjust the bracelet and necklace to your liking.  While I understand the reason behind their choice, I would have preferred both the necklace and bracelet to have the lobster clasp, but then only one ring to attach it to.To me it just added to how hard it is to put on the bracelet and necklace.  Maybe in part it's because I have longer nails, but it is kind of awkward to attach the closures with the different links, which there are several.I wear a size 8" in a bracelet, and this bracelet fits fine. In fact I closed the bracelet and just rolled it onto my wrist, otherwise I would never of gotten the bracelet attached on my own.The necklace I would say is about an 18" length.  It's not tight against your neck, but my personal preference for necklaces is like 24", to me it fits the way I want it to, also so I can just put the necklace over my head without having to open the closures.The necklace is just like the bracelet in that there are several links, so you can decide on how tight or how loose you want the necklace to be. At least the necklace was alittle easier for me to attach than the bracelet was.The earrings were the easiest for me to put on. The earrings have fish hook closures, which I don't really care for, but I was able to easily take off the fish hook part, and attach leverback closures, which is what I prefer as far as comfort, and not losing the earrings.The earrings do dangle alittle longer than I would have preferred.  More than 1 1/2" long, which is my personal preference.  I don't think they are 2" long tho.The bracelet, necklace and earrings as a set do look very nice together.  It's feminine, not chunky, so at least to me this set would be suitable to wear, whether you dress casual or dressy.  Normally I don't wear all of the same jewelry, meaning the same exact look, but this is interesting at least to me, to wear something as a set.  That being said, you don't have to wear the bracelet, necklace and earrings all at the same time, since the colors would work with more than one color, so you do have more than one option.So, I think most of my review is really a personal preference as far as how long you like your jewelry to be, how hard or easy you find different closures to be.The necklace, bracelet, and earrings as a whole seems to be very well made.I do wonder WHY tho if the necklace, bracelet and earrings are all sterling silver, than why are the links gold in color? There is nothing on any of the jewelry that is silver in color.All in all Iam very happy with this set.  My favorite color is blue, all shades of blue.  The lapis and turquoise do work well together with the gold links and closure.

review: First of all.If you got to a store, take a tape measure.So you know if the bag is 62 inches and legal.I took a luggage weight too (but it did not work).Some tags indicate the weight.So I compared it to a bag with known weight on the tag.Many spinner luggage in the soft side models weigh 12 to 13 pounds.Just got off the phone from Samsonsite.Apologize profusely.  MY review...which I will leave is for Samsonite Lig(H)t.I realized it when talking to them.The Lig(H)t is a special product made for certain stores.JUST so you know...you may not be getting a uber discount for the same products.Some stores have manufacturers make special products just for them.They look similar to other products. No way to compare.Since that product is NOT here because it is only sold at certain stores.And I already spent the time reviewing. And I have some info from Samsonite. I'll leave this.She also said many of those certain stores are related or have the same special productsmade for them and ONLY them. Proprietary  products makes it hard to comparison shop,to get accurate reviews and accurate product info.OK..I called Samsonite.I was looking inside and there was no zipper pull.Turns out, they do that on purpose so you don't use that space.Samsonite also told me the tag sewn inside the luggage is whatyou need for warranty and repair. (along with the receipt).So do leave the tag INTACT.You might want to copy down the numbers and have with yourreceipt for reference. Probably make repairs easier.My product has a ten year warranty.My product appears to have the same integrated handles as the LIFTThe front zippers are different than the LIFT.I'm guessing mine is made more cheaply.Sure it has the same $440 retail price tag!I bought the 29 inches LIG(H)T.It is 32 inches tall. 20 inches wide. & 10 inches thick without expanding the front pockets,thus it is the maximum allowable size.I liked this because it is around ten pounds. I liked the color, the integrated handles and the Samsonite name.This rolls better than one of the heavier Samsonites.I bought a new umbrella for a trip. It is 33 inches long.I bought this max allowable suitcase to hold the umbrella.These days your luggage is going to get damaged and will not last long.Especially is you max the weight...and buy these lighter cases.I have marked this five stars but it is a cheap piece of luggageand I have  no illusions it will last long.I've not field tested it yet.I spent hours looking at them. Most 29 inch suitcases run around 13 pounds.THE OFFICIAL tag on the bag says:Size  #  Dimension  Weight29" spinner  51233  29.5 x20  x10  10.8 lbs.  (Note it is 32 inches with wheels).Other Lig(H)t products:25" spinner  51232  25  x16.5 x9  9.3 lbs20" spinner  51231  20.5 x15  x8  7.4 lbs.It has a limited 10 year global warranty.SAM-HNGTG-2181The inside tag (sewn into the bag) saysSamsonite2013060545001575371541  (This is the code Samsonite needed to ID the bag)2013D100547This has no plastic reinforcers on the sides or the back.Some suitcases have nubs that stick out.There is a similar model to this with a zipper all across the back.The front zip picket goes all the way down. There is a second half size pocket.It does not have a removable cosmetic bag.It does not come with a lock.It does not have shoe holders.I consider it a basic BOX with wheels.I use packing things inside it.It does have two inside sections.But mostly a big open box.Now about the LIFT. Some reviews on the smaller sizes indicate a problema defect, etc.  Samsonite told me that is taken care of and happened along time ago. I was concerned because I was thinking about buying thematching cases to this...and did not want to buy at a discount place that had theold defective models. In that discussion I was told they selldifferent products, and I realized my review was for Lig(H)T not LIFT.IF you want a LIFT, make sure that is what it says on the tag.And now you know the rest of the story.
summary: Samsonite Lig(H)t very similar 29 inch and legal

review: I was about to travel overseas to Amsterdam which is known for steep steps and cobblestone streets. I only wanted to bring one bag, and liked the Rick Steves idea of going with only one carryon (although I cheated and also brought a messenger bag just in case).I love this suitcase for many reasons.PROS:- It's exactly carry-on size for most airlines, but only if it's lightly packed. Thus, I put a lot of stuff for the trip to Europe in my messenger bag so that the suitcase would stay 9" deep.- If you end up buying lots of souvenirs like I did on your trip, the bag actually expands in depth from 9" to 14". Of course, you'll probably have to check your bag in on the way back, but there will probably be no checked bag fee (for international travel, that is).- The suitcase straps are convenient if you need them. I personally never did, but I can see a situation where I don't really want to drag the bag.-It has a duffel-like strap over the top (when it's lying flat). You can't really use it for carrying, but it's nice to help heft a full bag onto your luggage rack for unpacking using just one hand.- You can use the adjustable side buckles to compress the bag a little more when it's packed, so that stuff moves around less and the bag takes up just a bit less spaceCONS:- As is typical, the collapsing bar you use to drag the bag takes up a lot of space in the interior.- Once full, the suitcase is pretty heavy on your back. It doesn't look like the materials use any of the super-fancy lightweight structural stuff like carbon fiber, but that's why you're saving money on luggage. :)- The grey and red version, which I bought, doesn't stand out like crazy on the conveyor belt, so you really gotta hunt for it for a flight coming off a 747 after you've gone through customs.- It's not a "spinner" four-wheeled suitcase. I'm still coveting one of those.All in all, I recommend this suitcase especially if you're traveling somewhere that you'll be coming home with more stuff than you left.
summary: This suitcase gives you more bang for the buck

review: I ordered the 2 pack of Burgundy and Dusty Rose in size Large, and they fit pretty much just right.  I am 5'6" tall, 140lb with a 36" chest, kind of wide shoulders and decent tone.  I ordinarily wear a size Medium or Small top (depending on designer). . .especially in this type of long, feminine cut T-shirt.  So it is true that they run a size or 2 small.  But they, like many good fashions, are meant to be FITTED (not tight/not baggy/just right), which they honestly are given your correct size match.  The material is good quality with ample stretch.  These T-shirts won't hide a muffin top when pulled down all the way smooth, but you can easily wear them in a way that they will (gather up/twist/stretch to desired shape).  What I am a little less than completely pleased about is the width of the sleeves and the cut of the collar.  The sleeves are definitely not too tight, but considering the fit of the body, they should be somewhat wider to leave just the SLIGHTEST gap between the sleeve opening and the topside of your arm.  Rather, the sleeve opening is completely flush all the way around your arm.  The cut of the collar is supposed to be a scoop neck, but it is really more like a crew neck with only a tad more depth.  To get those desired effects, I probably could have gone with a size X-Large and still kept the FITTED, feminine cut of these T-shirts due to the nature of the material.  I just don't want to.  I am debating whether or not to return them.  My favorite designer of this type of T-shirt is Femme (by Tresics).  In those, I wear a size Small. review: Posting now on this as a past purchase. I thought I would "upgrade" to a Pathfinder or Pro-Trek triple sensor model. Unfortunately, I found the barometer not to be useful for predicting weather, and I live in an area with very little elevation change.This watch skips the barometer/altimeter sensor, but retains the more useful compass and temperature sensor.Even if you don't use these two features at all, the watch is still a great value. The main display is very large and legible, and the sub-display shows the time of day even when you're in another mode. This is quite useful, when using the timer or stopwatch, for example. One can time one's activities and STILL see the time of day without having to switch modes.The backlight is not bright, but it doesn't really need to be, if you think about it. It lights up just the digits, but quite visibly in the dark.The buttons are big and easy-to-press, but the case is shaped so as to protect them from accidental actuation.The nylon band is extremely well executed. It is kind of stuff, but it is padded and sonically welded together. The holes for the buckle are melted through, so they won't stretch and cause the band to unravel. It is more comfortable than Casio's usual resin bands because nylon breathes with the wrist a bit better. Resin can be made to do this if shaped properly (and a lot of Casio bands are) but it doesn't take anything exotic on this. I'd like to see this band type on more Casios in the future.Over the last 30 years, I've had probably 30 digital Casios. Not because they need replacing, but because I'm always searching for that perfect feature set and comfort level. This one and the GD-350-1B are the top two so far. This one is thinner and has the compass, thermometer, and big display. The GD-350-1B has the Vibe Alarm and one-button access to the countdown timer from the home screen. (but is much thicker and twice the price)The one change I'd make to this watch would be to implement the thermometer sensor such that it can be use for instantaneous, on-the-wrist, temperature measurements. As it is now, I believe it uses the case back to stabilize readings, thus making it totally inaccurate unless it is taken off the wrist and left to sit somewhere for 20 minutes or so. As it is, it can still be useful, like for measuring different ambient temperatures in different places in the house. For example, in my apartment, I found that it can be 55 degrees in the bedrooms while it is 72 degrees near the thermostat. :-\
summary: Great display, great feature set, fair price. Time's visible in all other modes.

review: Like others have said, there is nothing cheap about this belt! The leather is thick but not stuffy or cheap and the studs are a nice muted antique bronze color, not shiny.I got it in dark red and it's a nice deep red, not too brown, not too tomato. So it did fit. However, imagine what that means - most of the front part of my torso had the front-most piece (the "strap" so to speak) going across it and the pretty wide pieces were mostly to the side and the back. So from the front it looked a lot just like a regular, single-width belt. This was a different look than the one in the product photo which shows the strap as mostly buckled in and the wider pieces prominently visible in the "flanks," if that makes sense. I thought for me to get the look in the photo required the larger belt - M/L so I contacted the seller and requested an exchange. They were super friendly and said no problem. A week later I have my replacement belt and it looks just the way I wanted it! So I recommend that if in doubt go with the larger size if you want the look in the photo. But if you are very very petite, go with the small one. Either way, customer service was so excellent you'll have no trouble exchanging! Thank you beltiscool!Highly recommend this product and seller and will order from them again.

review: I ordered the large size.  The bust/waist/hip measurements were spot on - no issue there.  The issues lie in the overall length and the length between components, so it just doesn't flatter like it does on the model (who I realize must be super short, or more likely wearing a custom made version).  The large size claims to suit someone 68" tall, though despite being just 2" taller, the bottom of the dress hits a whole 6" above my ankles when I wear the hip band where it's shown on the model.  Even less flattering, the top of the hip band and the bottom of the ribcage band are only 4" apart, so when the hip band is in the right place the ribcage band hits near my natural waist, and when I adjust it so the rib cage band is right under my chest, the hip band is at my waist.  I'm not longwaisted in the slightest, but realistically these bands should be more like 7-8" apart to fit their own size chart claim of a 68" tall woman.It's a bummer since this appears to be a fairly good quality costume overall.  The material isn't too thin and drapes pretty nicely, and the black and gold detail add a nice touch.  The style and design is quite sexy, and if you have the confidence to rock it you're halfway there.  I just really, really wish it fit like it should have.
summary: Proportions aren't right, especially if you're tall

It has two things working against it...first, Tungsten Carbide is extremely heavy, and secondly it is one of the hardest substances used in jewelry.  Combine those two attributes and this makes for one of the most uncomfortable rings to wear, especially every day.  Now, if you are buying this to wear occasionally, it might be bearable, because the ring does look good, doesn't scratch, and is a wide ring.  But because of its size, this will constantly hit and rub the neighboring fingers, and with the weight it made my ring finger constantly sore.  I actually thought I would get used to it and eventually it would not be painful, but after 9 months I gave up.  On a positive note, Ozeri was the seller and they were very helpful and a pleasure to buy from...they even sent me a second ring at no cost, in a different size, as a courtesy (I didn't ask for it, they just offered).  I wish this would have been more comfortable because it does look good...just not worth the pain!

review: I bought this to replace a Terry Alexander De La Rossa Robe.Men's Extra Thick Terry Cotton Hooded Bathrobewhich I love, but after two year needs a little mending.This came in and it turned out to be nothing more than a light sweat suit in the design of a robe.THERE IS NO TERRYCLOTH OR PURE COTTON absorbing material inside. I felt clammy wet spots and not dry. The Robe simply wasn't absorbent.Also the nap from the inside was shedding on me.(Cotton Fibers). I really wanted the robe to work so I figured that if I washed  and dried it twice it might make the material more absorbent and take out the nap. Sometimes clothes are treated with chemicals from the factory which effect absorption.After washing twice, the cotton nap shedding problem went away but no effect on absorbtionOne nice thing is that I bought the xxl and initially it was too long but after the washing it shrunk up an inch or two so if I had kept the robe I wouldn't have had to had it hemmed. (I'm 5'9" and the robe length would be fine for 5'9 and above.I'm returning the robe to Amazon. I was looking for a bathrobe that I could lounge around in and this only fits the lounging aspect.If you are looking for an absorbent bathrobe don't buy this.I was  amazed that the reviews on Amazon didn't bring this point up. This is one of the few times that I've bought a 1 star item on Amazon that had a rating in the high 4s.

review: I'm very pleased with this product. I initially was going to get a cover, but found that there's a pouch in my briefcase that's a perfect fit for the Touch. But, I still wanted to get something to protect the Touch, especially since I sometimes use it while snacking and have touched the Touch with sticky fingers (Danish and a Touch are not a good match, sorry to say. Thank goodness I use a stylus, or I would have made a real mess!).This is made of a substantial, rubber-type material. It's a perfect fit for the Touch - does not encroach upon the screen at all, and has cutouts for the ports, so it does not interfere with operation.The main benefit for me is an enhanced grip. It feels much more comfortable than the Touch alone, and gives me a better grip. Plus, if it gets dirty (or has a close encounter with Danish-enhanced fingers) it's easily removed for washing.Also good for using the Touch at the gym since I don't have to worry about slippage due to sweaty hands. It also keeps it secure and in place when using it on the treadmill reading rack.I imagine it would be great at the beach as well, but since it's presently December, that test will have to wait a few months.While it does offer protection for the Touch, it does not cover the screen. If your main goal is to protect the screen, this is not the right product - you'll want a case.I also appreciate the product packing. Very minimal packing waste - it comes wrapped in a thin plastic pouch which is in a paper envelope made of recycled paper. No box, which is great since this is not something that can break in transit. Granted, the product itself is (I would imagine) petroleum-based, but I like that Marware made an effort to keep waste to a minimum.Highly recommend.
summary: Helps keep the Touch safely in hand

What I like most about them is that I get a comfortable, athletic shoe feel without the casualness of tennis shoes. I even wear them to work.Updated 12/2013:Returned from Africa and they were an excellent choice!  Nice traction for walking on dirt paths, hills and uneven pavment and rocks; I got a lot of compliments from other travelers at how nice looking they are. review: People raved about the traction. I guess the traction is okay for people who are using the booties for exercising. For those of us who are in wheelchairs or who, like I, have paralyzed feet, the traction is NOT sufficient. For people such as I, traction means an entirely different thing -- and the issue of traction is more than a slight inconvenience. Lack of traction could mean a trip to the hospital, and it could mean life or death. So when I put my feet on our hardwood floors and they slid, this was NOT a happy thing. I had ordered four pairs, thinking these booties really didn't slide because reviewers said they didn't. I thought I had found a solution to a huge problem, because I had to wear Crocs to bed in case I had use the restroom in the night, and I really did not want to do so. Alas, I still have to wear Crocs to bed. Bummer.

review: I really like the functionality of the Ironman watches. I like this one in particular because it's on the smaller side. The watch features 3 alarms (with day-of-the-week options), 2 different count-down alarms (and one allows you to set multiple successive count downs of varying lengths), a stopwatch and 2 timezones. It's very easy to use, and gives instructions on how to set various things. I liked this watch so much that I bought a second after the first one broke.Unfortunately, every one of the Ironman watches I've owned have failed in the exact same way - the band. There are little rubber tabs that fit into the watch case, and they are the only thing that connects the band to the watch. If the rubber tabs get stretched or torn, then the watch case falls out. To be fair, the band is definitely comfortable, and the curvature of the band makes it fit sturdily without being too tight.The bands have lasted between 3-14 months, so I've been buying a new $30-$40 watch once a year (or so) for the past 6 years. I've been unable to find watch band replacements, and I'm not sure that I could do it myself anyway. The warranty specifically says that it does not cover any problems with the band of the watch. At this point, there's a clear financial incentive for Timex to pair awesome watches with bad bands. If you search the reviews for Ironman watches, so many people just buy a new watch when the watchband breaks, rather than try to get the band replaced. I'm boycotting the Ironman watches because of this.It looks like the Timex Expedition has most of the features I want, so that's what I'm going to get next.

review: When I received this watch my initial impression was that it was excessively huge for a woman.  It seemed better designed for a man's wrist.  However, I've come to appreciate this watch as a piece to add a bit of pizazz to a relatively simple outfit.  While it can command positive attention in the right circumstances, I wouldn't consider it suitable as an everyday watch.What I found lacking about the watch:1) The watch is very difficult to have sized.  It took me seven hours over the course of several days to get this thing sized.  I went to three jewelers that each sold a wide assortment of watches and was told by each that they lacked the tool to size the watch.  I was told to take it back to my Invicta retailer.  Invicta's site lacks a retailer listing so I went to google and searched for "invicta+retailer" and came across a forum where people were trying to find retailers to get their watch sized.  I ended up at J.C. Penney and they sized the watch.2) This watch is heavy.  If you type a lot and suffer from wrist strain, I don't see this as the watch for you. I have strong wrists and this watch made my wrists ache on the days I wore it.3) The ceramic on the watch is easily marred.  I am in a relatively low-impact office setting but by the end of the day, my watch was covered by black streaks. I was able to wipe most of the scuffs and marks away with a shirt, but it has added a bit of upkeep that I didn't anticipate.What do I like:1) I like the appearance and that counts for a lot.  I never anticipated that I would be able to wear a watch that was this big.  However, it works well with simple outfits.  If you like jeans and cardigans, this watch can add an adornment that will hold its own.2) The face is large.  I don't have poor eyesight but the large numbers are nice even for my eyes.  I don't have to bring my wrist closer to my face to easily see the time.3) The watch appears to be well-made.4)Other than size, it is simple in its design.  There are no rhinestones or diamonds (real or fake) or other properties that make the watch overly tacky.I would recommend the watch but not as a watch for everyday use.  It is a watch that you wear a couple of times a month.  It is not suitable for those with weak wrists or those that cannot find a retailer to have the watch sized.  From my experience, you cannot expect your regular jeweler to be able to remove the links unless they sell Invicta watches.
summary: it's attractive but it is a pain to have sized and it is marred easily

piece to add a bit of pizazz to a relatively simple outfit while it can command positive attention in the right circumstances i wouldnt consider it suitable as an everyday watchwhat i found lacking about the watch the watch is very difficult to have sized it took me seven hours over the course of several days to get this thing sized i went to three jewelers that each sold a wide assortment of watches and was told by each that they lacked the tool to size the watch i was told to take it back to my invicta retailer invictas site lacks a retailer listing so i went to google and searched for invictaretailer and came across a forum where people were trying to find retailers to get their watch sized i ended up at jc penney and they sized the watch this watch is heavy if you type a lot and suffer from wrist strain i dont see this as the watch for you i have strong wrists and this watch made my wrists ache on the days i wore it the ceramic on the watch is easily marred i am in a relatively lowimpact office setting but by the end of the day my watch was covered by black streaks i was able to wipe most of the scuffs and marks away with a shirt but it has added a bit of upkeep that i didnt anticipatewhat do i like i like the appearance and that counts for a lot i never anticipated that i would be able to wear a watch that was this big however it works well with simple outfits if you like jeans and cardigans this watch can add an adornment that will hold its own the face is large i dont have poor eyesight but the large numbers are nice even for my eyes i dont have to bring my wrist closer to my face to easily see the time the watch appears to be wellmadeother than size it is simple in its design there are no rhinestones or diamonds real or fake or other properties that make the watch overly tackyi would recommend the watch but not as a watch for everyday use it is a watch that you wear a couple of times a month it is not suitable for those with weak wrists or those that cannot find a retailer to have the watch sized from my experience you cannot expect your regular jeweler to be able to remove the links unless they sell invicta watches

review: So I'm just going to skip a lot of the babbling and go for the kill. THIS SHIRT SUCKS! I did give it 2 stars because of the design and cheap price. I paid roughly $7 bucks for this and that's going to include shipping. I also want to note that this Review for this shirt, does not reflect on the "Amazon Seller." With that last part being said. The seller of this item, shipping wise, awesome. So was the packaging and product information.I'll get technical here throughout this review. I'm just trying to go with what flows or comes to mind first. I ordered the size Small and that would be in USA size 4. I'm normally not a small in anything. I don't have a big chest, my bra size is a 32B, pants depend on who makes them for instance, I'm a Size 9 Average in Wal-mart brand L.E.I and (Wal-mart brand also) No Boundaries Size 13. Like I said, It just depends on the brand. My overall body type would be "Mom Average." And what I mean by that is- I've had kids and I'm no longer in my prime, in addition, I bare the loving "Mom Marks" across my entire body. So loose skin, cellulite, muffin top and a gut is a constant. no matter how hard you work out or eat healthy. Unless I have surgery I've gotta deal with what I've got!Sorry, back to the shirt. It fit perfect. Helped conceal my stomach area well and fit me close to the model just a bit tighter on the stomach area. The material is that of a plain Hanes t-shirt. It does have the lace on the the shoulders and a small upper part of the back area (Like where the tag would go.) The design is made of an almost plastic type material (Like an Iron on letter, numbers or name would be on a Jersey.) This is usual with most Print shirts. When I unfolded the shirt after taking it out of the plastic it WAS stuck together but the Image itself stayed intact and didn't come off.I wore it for a few hours, around 6-8 hrs. Doing regular things like shopping, paying bills, cooking dinner... and so on. I washed this on the lowest and gentlest cycle on my washing machine, which would be the Lingerie Cycle. Along with a few bra's and undergarments in Cold Water. I then put it into the dryer on Low Heat which would be Fluff Air, for my dryer. Fluff Air would mean that its like if you use the Hair Dryer and put it on the Cold Setting then it would basically blow air that's ventilated from whatever your room temperature is. I've never had a problem with using this cycle it only take the stuff, depending on size and material a few times to dry. I usually let it go once and then pull the garment out damp and let it finish by hang drying. I did this with the shirt and when I went back (because I didn't notice anything when I put it on the hanger to dry or when I was pulling it out of the washer or taking it out of the dryer,) that the material stained the design. The skull stayed intact to the shirt but it has noticeable black spots seeping throughout the entire design making it look like it was wet and got mildew. The lace part also had  a ton of elastic poking everywhere around the sewn parts (like sewn from the cloth of the shirt and the lace) around the arms, neck, chest and back area. That's why I gave it such low stars. No where on the the shirt or on the product description does it state how to wash the garment. Nor does it tell you how to properly care for it. I'm not sure if it was just because I stuck it in the washer or the dryer, maybe its suppose to be hand washed with a mild detergent? Either way, I am disappointed!Buyer's beware and you can choose to take the plunge or not. I have ordered another shirt from this same seller and the same company that this shirt is from. And I've had bad results with it as well.

Now, I'm here to order another pair, and I think that this time I'll try ordering two sizes up, and see if that stops the rolling problem.  I will save the second, damaged pair as a backup for the wedding day.  Those two issues caused me to take away two of the five stars that these could potentially be (for me, anyway).  One more thing... as it stands now, I couldn't imagine wearing these every day... it really is a GIRDLE!  But for a special occasion, or a night out on the town, yessiree!!  They do really suck you in and give you that figure you wish you had! (Maybe not recommended for going out to dinner, if you plan to eat more than a salad LOL!)

review: That one star reviewers don't seem to actually read reviews...or some of them anyways?  Most people going through these reviews have discovered that the are at least a half size small.  And they are.  But is that a reason for 1 star?  Really?  Anybody who buys shoes at roadrunnersportsdotcom will know that they ALWAYS recommend a half size larger for most shoes.  So, I was not surprised and order the 12 4e instead of 11.5.  And they fit well.  I love the generous toe box on these shoes.  The support and motion control are pretty good too.  All in all the price for these shoes on Amazon is lower than elsewhere...I do know they were recently on sale for low low low, but now the price is back where it was...$10 and more higher most other places.  I have paid substantially more and it is always a drag when you discover that your price shoes are in need of replacing, so I was very pleasantly surprised with how well these performed at the current price point. I tried another pair that were even less, but they had no support, so I will use them for indoor biking, etc.  I know each manufacturer has their fans and I am an NB fan for sure.  I still don't get the 1 star thing though...people just being people I imagine.

review: I own 12 of these UA Tech shirts and for the most part, I love 'em!  What's the reason I don't give them 5 stars?  After multiple washings and dryings, the fabric tends to pill.Another reason I give it less than the full 5 stars, even if you wear deodorant, but sweat while working out, these shirts will (again, over time) retain odors - no matter what laundry detergent you use.  NOTE:  I know this may be just my own special breed of chemicals that has done this to my UA Tech shirts, but take it as an FYI if you, like me, sweat a lot during workouts.  If you're buying these for looking cute around town, the retained odor should be no issue at all.Overall, these are well made, comfortable, loose fit UA shirts which are lightweight and perfect for working out or hanging out in warm weather. They don't shrink or lose their shape. (I have a 40" chest and purchase Large in these tees, wearing a compression bra underneath.)  The colors are vibrant and the price is on par with similar garments from other makers.Because the fabric is fairly thin, my bra does show through with some colors.  Take this into consideration when purchasing and/or wearing different colored bras underneath these tees.

review: I don't like digital watches. I'm a chronograph guy. James Bond never wore a digital. American astrononauts don't wear digitalis. But after 30 years of wearing chronograph watches (even very expensive ones) I decided that I needed a watch that wouldn't crap out on me after swimming a few a months worth of laps in the local YMCA pool.I don't EVER take my watches off. EVER! Not in the pool, not in the hot tub, not in the shower, not at work as a machine technichian, not huntining, not sleeping, not EVER! (the one exception being when I work out on my heavy bag. A watch just won't fit under the wraps.)My point is, a watch for me has to be there for me 24/7. I've spent thousands of dollars on certified dive watches (even ones designed for NAVY SEALS (don't waste your money.) and none of them. NONE of them have held up like this watch has.I wanted to own this watch for at least a year before I gave it a review. I have. So here it is in pro vs. con format.PROIt may be bulletproof. (I haven't shot a bullet at it yet but this is one tough mother%^<>€ watch.)I've had it a year and it looks like I just took it out of the box. (not a single scratch or ding to the crystal.)The functions were easy to learn even for a non digital user.I guess it's a pretty watch if you like digital type watches (it's just functional as far as I'm concerned.)The time and date are large (black on gray) and easy to read even for the near sighted.The function buttons are both a pro and a con. They're large enough and require enough tension to easily depress underwater yet at the same time are impossible to operate with heavy winter mittens on.The auto light is also a pro and con. It does work. It's just VERY particular in how you hold your arm and tilt your hand and for how long. But it does work. (either way you can shut it off with the touch of a button)I like the clip at the end of the wristband. It keeps the band from coming loose during more strenuous activities. (not a big deal but I like it.)Speaking of bands, I'm not sure what this one is made of but it's tough. It's not not like a regular watch band that can be replaced with one from any Wal-Mart. This is integrated into the watch.It always radio/auto/atomic updates. The signal is broadcast from ft.Collins,Co. I'm in S MN. I work from home from midnight til noon. My office is in the basement of our home and my watch has never once missed an update signal.CONSShe's thick! Twice as thick as a good dive watch. It get's hung up on shirts and jackets and such. Really not a big deal.As I said above, impossible to operate the function buttons with gloves on.Also, as I said above, the auto light can be particular.But let's get down to the ONLY reason I didn't give this watch 5 stars.That reason is the three sub dials. I don 't know what the Casio engineers in Japan were smoking the day they designed this watch but they screwed the pooch on These! The largest subdial isn't even covered in the user manual. I had to go online to find out what it does and what it does is useless. The middle subdial shows the watches charge and the last and smallest (tiny) dial shows all of the vital functions of the watch. (four different alarm settings, snooze,DST,signal,split times) and more. These readouts are the most important readouts of the watch and they're tiny! My eyes are fine and I can't read them.I almost sent it back just because of the subdials but I just use it for time and date and this watch hasn't let me down.Her Majesy's Secret Service And NASA can afford to equip their agents with new cool expensive watches after every mission, but I'm just a middle class guy and this watch has performed in everything from -28F to 108F. From 30m below sea level  to 22,000 ft above sea level. Through countless laps, thousands of hours of work and a lot of showers. (probably not enough for my wife and her friends.) it's been more than a year and this watch rocks the house!!!If you want a good reliable bullet proof watch this is the one. (if you you live up North this way, ya may have to throw it under a light every once in a while in the winter when the sun don't come out.)

review: I used to be a runner and would compete almost every weekend in races up to and including the marathon. I always had to select my shoes carefully in that I was an overpronator and would develop sore ankles. Finally, after running for over 20 years, I stopped. Well, now I'm 77 and I've put on too much weight and needed more exercise. Running cam to mind. So, I went back to walking but I could feel my ankles hurting after a couple miles. It was not a good sign. Then I remembered running shoes and how they had all sorts of models for all the known feet problems. I went to a nearby store and explained my difficulties and my goals (a 5 K race).The knowledgeable clerk suggested the Brooks Beast '12 Running Shoe. I liked it immediately. It was large and heavy, but that was what I needed. The fit and feel were perfect. They were expensive, but running is a very inexpensive sport and it pays to put your money in good shoes. So  far, I've just been walking in them. The feel great! I'm now up to three-mile walks and there's no problems at all. My ankles feel fine, in contrast to the soreness associated with my walking shoes. I plan to gradually increase my distance and speed. Then I'll add intermittent jogging. Finally, I'll jog the entire three miles. The gradual increase should take a couple months. At that point I'll be ready for that 5K run. With these shoes, it should be no problem.Gary Peterson

review: I read the reviews after I bought the dress, and I won't lie to you, I was absolutely terrified that I had just wasted my money on a dress I could never wear. Just got it in, and the shipping was really much faster than expected. Now, I'm going to give you all a 411 on this dress, or at least the one I received.The one I got is EXACTLY like the one in the image. It is chiffon, or close enough not to matter honestly. The polka dots are the same as well, as is the zipper on the side and the elastic on the arms. Now, I got mine in a large because I run well into the busty side and I know that I have to go up a size or two depending on the fabric. This has no give or flex, so you definitely should go up a size if you're top heavy like me.Just a 411- I'm 5'8 inches at 165 lbs, medium bone and running to the curvier side of things and really busty. I am by no means thin at all so I can say I speak from at least the middle range of female forms.Now, here is what I didn't get. I saw a lot of comments about the top being open and needing a shirt underneath to keep your boobs from being a bit too out there. I had no such problem at all actually, it's a sweet little cross over and I flash no breasts what so ever. I also saw comments about the collar, again I have no issue with it. It's a fold over one, pretty typical with this sort of crossover. It sits perfectly flat for me so I don't see how it can be an issue for others? I saw a lot of issues with the sleeves and I can tell you that I have no problem with them whatsoever. They weren't too tight for me and I got big arms, so really, not sure what's going on there.  The length was fine for me and I got a toosh, and it covered me up just fine. Came about an inch or two above my knees, still perfectly decent to wear out for many occasions. The see through thing... Now I have to admit that the lining on it is waaay to thin to provide any sort of real coverage. So yes, a slip would be a wise idea when wearing this dress. As for the top part, when I tried it on I was wearing a black bra, and it didn't show at all. Was just the bottom that gave me a bit of an issue.I will say that the way the dress falls is not as open or...free flowing? As the one in the image, but again, I'm a bigger girl so I tend to fill my stuff out a bit more than the more willowy figures. I think wearing a pannier with this dress would be FANTASTIC! Would fill it out a bit and give it a very cute shape. I chose to wear mine with a thick belt because they are right about the ruching about the middle. It's not...very flattering with my shape and it draws attention rather than disguises. With the belt though, it's totally adorable and really draws the eye to the curves.Overall, this dress totally deserves the five stars I'm giving it. I do feel bad for anyone who got the short end of the stick with it because I am thoroughly happy with my purchase. If your busty, buy it larger. Better to dart it in yourself or have it taken in, than have it be too small. I'll be tailoring mine in a bit soon about the waist, but it's not a major necessity. Honestly, very cute dress and at the price? Why not give it a go?

review: Love this ring. Have worn it for several months on my right middle finger and it's a great high quality of sterling silver. I wear it with another sterling silver ring with some smoky topaz. You can certainly stack it with other rings, but it is wide, so keep that in mind. Someone said something about it not staying shinny, but it's supposed to be worn and yes, it can lose it's luster, which doesn't take anything a way from the look of this ring. I've learned with experience that rings like this will get naturally shinny again. I love the wave pattern of the ring, and most of all, I like the fact that it's unique. I don't want to be wearing the same thing as everyone else. I get lots of compliments on it. It's look good as a thumb ring as well. Great value for a solid sterling silver ring!

review: I wanted "toe shoes" for awhile and originally wanted the 5-toed versions, but they were rather costly. So, after looking around a little bit here, I spotted these nice Fila Skele-toes slip-on 4-toed shoes at a lower price. Since they looked nice and were decently priced, I decided to get 2 pairs of these shoes meant for walking outside or wearing them in dirt or mud while hiking. They are sometimes a bit hard to get on since you got to guide your toes into the toe slots on these shoes, but not bad as I now have a silly system of getting into these shoes faster now. They are rather comfy to wear and I can wear them all day with no problems. They are good in water and mud, tho not much traction is on these shoes, but they seem to do well enough. These are not running shoes, but good enough for walking outside for a walk or a light hike. Since they are water-resistant, they go thru water, dirt and mud just fine. I may purchase more of these shoes soon since I have just about worn out the first pair already and now working on my second pair of these shoes. Their sizing seems to run the same with sneakers, so I got the same size or a half a size larger just in case they ran small. I like these shoes.

review: I purchased these slippers based on all the rave reviews.  Also, I checked the UGG website to confirm that Orva Stores was an authorized retailer.  When I opened the box, they seemed fine.  They are comfy and cozy.  However, when I saw the box insert about how to check authenticity of the shoe, of course I had to check.  There is a QR code tag sewn into the left shoe.  It also has a line of stars that are suppose to turn from black to white when you turn the tag 90 degrees.  There is also a QR code tag on the outside of the box.  I scanned the code on the "box" and it took me directly to the UGG website saying it was authentic.  I then checked the tag inside the shoe, and noticed that the numbers/letters beside the QR code did not coincide with the numbers beside the code on the box.  This made me a little suspicious.  I then scanned the code inside the shoe...and it came up as an invalid scan.  I thought maybe it was a fluke, so not only did I scan it again...but tried 3 different QR code scan apps and they all came out the same.  Invalid.  I have a problem with paying full price for a product that isn't authentic.  If they had been priced at say even $50, I would have been fine.  However, I contacted Amazon.com and they are issuing me a full refund based on my description of the problem.  All I can say is...buyer beware.  If you want authenticity...it's usually best to go straight to the source...or a well known retailer such as Belk, Dillards, REI, etc.PLEASE NOTE:  According to the purchase process, it says that it is sold by Orva Stores, but shipped from Amazon...so there is not way to know exactly where the unauthentic product came from.  It could have come from the Amazon Warehouse...unknowingly to either party.

review: I collect watches of all types. I've eyed this watch for some time, and finally pulled the trigger on it. For the price, this is an amazing buy.Just a rundown:-CURRENT TIME IN ALL SCREENS: this is HUGE. So many watches do not show the current time in the countdown timer or stopwatch screen, and that's extremely frustrating. This watch shows the current local time in ALL screens except the database (understandably). So you can use the functions of this watch and still use it as a watch. that's great.-Countdown timer: Countdown timers should be a standard feature of ALL digital watches. Current time, alarm, stopwatch and CDT used in conjunction with each other allow you to measure the passage of time in every way you could possibly need to, which is the entire purpose of a wristwatch.-Database feature: Many people have said "i don't need this feature because everyone has a cellphone these days". That's true, but really think about it... you don't have to plug this watch in and charge it every day, and it's not going to get ruined if you drop it in the mud or get it wet. If it's strapped to your wrist, you're probably not going to accidentally leave it somewhere. anyone who has ever had their phone die or get unexpectedly lost/broken should recognize the value of having important numbers stored in a more robust device as a backup.-Cost: Any watch that keeps accurate time and costs less than a night at the movies is a steal. Especially this one, since it does so much more than that.-Alarms: You have 5, one is a snooze. As far as i can tell, there's no weekends/weekdays function, but you can set them to go off every day, every day of a particular month, a certain day of every month (ie: the 5th of the month every month.), or on a specific day of a specific month (ie: every june 5th @ 6pm).-Looks: It's sort of goofy looking. But it's a $15 black plastic Casio. I mean, really, what are you expecting here?-Other stuff: I can't speak to battery life. i seriously doubt it will last 10 years. If it lasts 2 years it'll be worth the $buy it. If you don't like it, skip lunch tomorrow and you've basically broken even.
summary: A lot of watch for very little money.

review: I'm going to NYC for the holidays, and as a native of Southern California, I definitely don't own a winter coat! I bought this because it seemed like a great value with pretty positive reviews. I am 5'3", 130lbs, relatively petite and I typically buy S/M. I'm not really familiar with winter coats, so I decided to go with the M. Once again, I'm not familiar with winter coats (how tight/loose/roomy you want your coat to be when it's NYC cold), but the medium was definitely too large for me. I felt like a marshmallow. I exchanged it for a small... and that still felt a bit roomy! I was tempted to try for an XS, but I never wear XS. I figure the extra room will provide space for layers. Also, the buckle-thing in the back didn't give me very much shape, so I unbuckled it and tied it instead... it actually looks like I have a waist now!As for warmth, I can't really say until I return from NYC, but it's thicker(yet light) and nicer than any jackets I own. The pockets seemed pretty warm too! Might update when I return

review: I have bought thousands of dollars of nursing bras in my 8 years of nursing, but Anita are the best, and I wish I had discovered them sooner.  The sizing is simple and dead-on, the nursing clips are easy to use, the fabrics never itchy or too bumpy to wear under clothes and they last.  This bra is very functional and not sexy, but I don't care about that - I just want it to lift, separate, and not drive me nuts all day.  This bra does all that and is a reasonable price (for those of us with big cup sizes who are used to paying $80+ per bra.)  Details: no sliding nursing reminder, but I never used that anyway on my other Anita bra - I just feel myself up if I cannot remember; enough stretch in the fabric to allow for fluctuations in fullness, but doesn't stretch out over the day; standard three-tightness adjustment in back; stretchy fold over edging and full-cut means no quadra-boob.

review: These aren't jeggings and they aren't leggings and they aren't jeans.  They are an amalgamation of all three. Styled like a jean, they are made of really soft, thick (winter-weight) stretchy, cotton-blend fabric.  I bought the plum purple color thinking it would be more of an aubergine, but instead it's more of a very saturated, rich dark purple--which I think is attractive, especially since I hate the color purple.  They are a great color for breaking up the all-black look I tend to fall back on when I don't want to think about styling an outfit. I usually wear a 10 so I bought size large. I'm 5'7" 138 lbs with very long legs. They run big! If you're between sizes, definitely order down. I will be ordering the other colors I plan to buy in a medium These are just the right full length (They cover my ankle) but they easily roll up to capris. . They are very stretchy and they lose their shape a bit after the first wearing, but I was able to get two wears out of them.  The tag says dry clean only or hand wash, but I washed mine in cold water (front loader) and line-dried them with no shrinkage and no ironing. I must however wear them with a belt or they fall down.  I love the fit--not as skin-tight as a jegging and not thin like a legging, these are fitted through the thighs/knees but they don't strangle your ankles. They are substantial enough to wear on their own but are fitted enough to wear under a short dress or tunic without looking bulky.  And best of all, they arrived beautifully packaged in pink tissue paper with a little note saying thanks for the purchase and a coupon for $'s off my next purchase.  Excellent delivery and that extra touch was well-appreciated.  It made it feel like I was unwrapping a gift.  Classy.
summary: Now I understand why the name of these pants is so long!

review: New Balance is continuing to irk customers by making unnecessary changes to good products.  I compare my 846 to my old 845 and I'm pretty disappointed with what New Balance has done.  The 846 has a more pointy toe so the toe box is more narrow and less comfortable than the old 845.  For an athletic/walking shoe silly style should never compromise comfort and performance.  The shoes have a slightly higher rise on the rear that causes a mild irritation on my Achilles tendon, the old 845 did not.  New Balance is the only game in town because I have not been able to find another company that makes a size 12 1/2 in a double AA width, so I kept them because I need something to wear.  I would prefer a black shoe, unfortunately white is the only color available in my size.  The 846 is not unbearable but could have and should have been much better.
summary: New Balance Men's MW846, a step in the wrong direction

review: I struggle with wanting to wear these boots on long hiking trips as they often leave my ankle bones very bruised and tender for days. On one long hike, I had to take the boot off the right foot and hike out barefoot because my ankle was in so much pain. These boots have a firm, protruding, padding that is snug against my ankle, presumably for support, but causes bruising on the bone from the pressure.  I have tried loosening the boot near the top, but that just makes my foot slide in the boot. I've only been able to use these boots by cutting up an old neoprene knee brace and stuffing it in my sock to cover my ankle.  It doesn't completely alleviate the pain, but I am able to hike longer than I could without the neoprene. I've worn these on over 20 hiking trips and they are still not fully broken in to where I can wear them and not experience pain. Considering the cost of these boots, I am not a happy customer. Wish that shoes were returnable after being worn. I certainly would have returned these if I could. Unfortunately, you can't find the problems without wearing them, and then you can't return them.
summary: Needs A LOT of breaking in

review: Ok. I have worn many combat related socks. I may have ranted and raved about a few for keeping me warm. I stick by those comments.However finding the right fitting sock can be a challenge, especially if you are tall and have strong athletic legs. Many socks claim over the calf. It would be safe to say that many socks are rarely over the calf. However, the Under Armour boot sock is well over the shin bone and almost to the kneecap. What is amazing about this particular sock is that is holds up all day long. No sagging at all. Very impressive.
