review: This past summer was a horrible summer for any sunglasses I touched. I went through about a dozen cheap pairs that I picked up from local brick and mortar stores. Sitting here I realize that, if I were to add up the cost of all these cheap sunglasses, I could of bought a couple of very nice pairs. I have only owned cheap sunglasses and I wondered if paying a premium on sunglasses is worth it.Enter the Oakley Mens Crosshair sunglassesI picked these up because they came to my attention being apart of a sale. I was hesitant due to even the sale price but, as I mentioned beforehand, just a few cheap pairs would cover the cost of these glasses.They arrived in the mail and come pre-packaged in a simple cardboard box. Within the cardboard box the glasses are protected in a soft cloth bag and also contain a cleaning rag and small info brochure.I immediately pulled out the glasses and spent the next few hours walking around trying them on and, have to say, I am pretty impressed.Many of my cheap glasses broke due to poor construction of the frames. These frames feel durable and like they will last. I have a large head that does put tension on glasses. These glasses were able to take the tension yet retain their original shape.The glasses are not polarized but do a good job on their own protecting my vision from the suns glare. The lenses are the perfect tint and did not block out my vision of the outside world.I received compliments from my wife and neighbors about how stylish the glasses look. It reminds me of the hoopla during the 80's when Tom Cruise sported "Aviator" glasses from the movie Top Gun and the craze he created.PRO:++ Durable construction++ Compliments on how stylish they lookCON:++ For price, only receiving a cloth bag for protection is cheapCONCLUSION:These glasses are well worth the premium paid over regular sunglasses you find sitting on a carousel rack at the local brick and mortar stores. Just watch for the sales when they go on to pick up then.
summary: Sleek, stylish, and durable glasses that the ladies (and I) love!

review: A long time ago I had knee surgery and the surgeon told me to wear "Tens" hose to keep the swelling down. Then I though why not try wearing the compression hose in the first place? Like I suspected my legs felt much better post ride if I wore the compression hose during the ride.Fast forward - one of my girls has sore feet after practice. Why not give compression socks a try? I bought my daughter this brand and another brand to try. She loved them both. If she wears them during practice her feet don't hurt after practice.At $20 a pair these are a great value.

review: I bought this hat a little over a year ago for the coming trout season.  I finally had to put my old hat into retirement after it was practically in pieces.  Most fishing hats, rods, gear etc, are steeped in a lot of tradition and superstition, thus we tend to hang onto things such as hats for as long as we possibly can.  When I decided to buy this one here on Amazon, I admit I was taken in mostly by the price.  I had seen hats very similar to this in places like American Eagle Outfitters and Old Navy for easily twice the price they are asking for it here.  I was pleasantly surprised upon receiving it by the high quality and comfort.  It is a very handsome hat that is extremely comfortable, and functional as well. And the quality of the fabric and stitch is superior considering the price. The hat has too heavy snaps on the side for folding the side brims up for the times when you want to look cool, and a nice rawhide drawstring to keep you from having to chase it down river in the event of an unexpected gust.  I felt as if it had been part of my fishing trips all along from the very first outing with it.  One thing that should be cleared up however is its claim of being water repellent.  This hat is not water repellent but rather weather resistant.  Meaning it won't repel water, but it will not be affected by it either.  This has been rained on, dropped in the water, and been thoroughly soaked and it has neither lost any of its shape or quality feel.  It may get wet if there is enough water, but it is not the least bit at any loss for it after drying out, (which it does very quickly). If you truly want something water repellent, you will just have to move up to a more expensive, (and in my experience more uncomfortable), hat that truly has a repellent lining.  In my opinion unless you love fishing in driving rainstorms or the feel of anything at all wet on your head, I would recommend this fine quality hat.  It is very comfortable, handsome and functional, and comes in a wide variety of colors and sizes that are sure to fit just about anyone.  I love it and feel that its an excellent deal.  Highly recommended...

review: This item has quite a good feel to it when first using it, and doesn't feel very "plastic-y" like most of the other tools out there, but from there the praise ends.The height adjustment tool is a notched springboard at the end of the tool.  The purpose of this being that if you've got a thicker band, you can press down on it to align it with the pin.  The problem with this system is that it causes the band to be unstable, and if it slips on the surface of that spring board, the pin will bend and snap off.Also, the pins included with this tool, while longer than most, are the flimsiest item I've ever seen.  I realize these pins are supposed to bend to protect your watch under too much pressure, but minimal pressure not only caused them to bend but snap completely!  I went out and bought another link remover, frustrated with this one's flawed design, and it worked much better.

review: I wore this boot for training for a 200 mile hike on the Camino de Santiago. It was perfect . . .comfortable, waterproof on rainy days and walking through puddles, nice looking even. I bought a second pair for the hike itself, identical except that it is the wine color. I do not like to use a boot more than 500 miles because it compacts and gives me less support. So, after about 400 miles in the purple pair, I switched to the wine pair, trained for about 100 miles in them, and wore them on trail. They were equally comfortable. However, the interior material around my ankle was leather in the wine-color boot whereas it had been cloth in the purple one. The yellow leather bled onto my wool socks when I sweated . . . all the time, of course. And the exterior leather bled onto my pant legs when it was wet, which was quite often because we managed to pick a rainy several weeks for our trip. Neither of these problems affected performance, but I would have preferred that it didn't happen. The one day when it rained so much that I had to walk through standing water several times, the boots got wet all the way through. That was a performance problem for me and I was lucky that we only had one such day. I wonder if my purple boots would have bled or gotten wet through. During my training walks they were not put to such a tough test as the wine-color boots had to face on the trail. Over all, I really liked the boots and would recommend them.

Its has the same feature list as the GW5000 but its much more wallet-friendly and easier to be replaced (in case your destoried it). So IMO, GW5000 is still top of the food chain, but when I am working on my car, guess which one I will be wearing. :)BTW, I swapped the original GWM5610 strap with the one from GW5000. The GW5000 strap is much softer and much more comfortable. Definitely a plus.What I like about it:+ classic 5600 style+ comfortable to wear 24/7 (especially with the GW5000 strap)+ 24hr STW and CDT (a definite upgrade from GWM5600 with 60 min CDT)What I don't like about it:- no current time in both STW and CDT (its my complain for most atomic solar G-Shock less a few that has 3 line of display)- this is a picky one but the in current time display mode in the GW6900, you can display the 2nd timezone. review: This is a beautiful coat,  not too thick,  removeable bib, double breasted with a single inner big button at the inside flap and big buttons at the front. It is surprisingly warm for such a medium thickness coat.However, sizing is a problem with this coat; here's my info:I'm 5'9", 200lbs. But once I got the XL size coat I soon found out that for this coat in particular, chest size is the most important. The XL was too tight around my shoulders and upper back and I literally could not do a hugging motion, it felt as if the coat could rip. My chest measures 47" (chest alone) and 53" ( arms included). The sizing info provided in this coat's page is NOT for this specific coat, so don't base your purchase on it. Do the following:Measure your chest in two ways:1. Raise your arms and measure around your chest at its thickest section.2. Lower your arms and measure around your chest,  at the same level as before, but this time including your arms (a bit bellow the shoulders); this measurement shouldn't be more than 10" greater than the measurement from step 1; if it is,  go a size larger.Hope this helps.
summary: Very Nice Coat....Measure Chest Before Buying.

review: The way that Amazon groups "identical" items with color options definitely failed on this one, but that is VERY obvious from the pictures. It wasn't obvious from the pictures which type was laid out which way, so I ordered two different ones, the Olive drab and the tan w/ radioactive symbol. I figured for the price, I could just return whichever I didn't like. Ended up keeping and LOVING both, for different reasons:Olive Drab:Has no inner dividers, but has a small "security pocket" inside, big enough for a checkbook plus pens and a few flash drives.Higher quality fabric, VERY STURDY strap, which is also adjustable, not padded, but wide.  Lots of inside space, 4 pockets, each big enough for a large water bottle, but the pockets are kinda weird, stuff in them is not really secure, feels like little stuff might fall out.Heavy. Higher quality canvas makes it heavier, but if you need it to carry a laptop plus 3-5 hardcover text books, you probably don't care about weight. This is the one you want.Ended up using this as a camera bag for my camera kit, the pockets are perfect for lenses, and I have 4 primary lenses so it worked out. Love the ample space inside, no crazy "features" to get my hands caught in when I'm in a hurry, but obviously I had to "craft" a cradle for my DSLR body to rest in. the fabric quality is just as good as any of the $100+ "journalist" or "reporter" bags, and it actually looks very similar as well. (also, space for a laptop)Tan w/ Radioactive Logo2 inner dividers = 3 inner sections, each big enough for a laptop in a thick neoprene sleeve, with some slop. (One is completely separated off, the other two are 1 big pocket w/ a fixed divider.)Strap is kinda wimpy. The metal fixings appear durable enough, might decide to replace the clasps that actually clip to the bag, as this clasp design can bend over time, but they seem pretty good. The strap itself is maaaybe an inch thick, and the sliding pad for your shoulder isn't really padded. Also it's not very long. Most men won't be able to cross it over their head to rest on the opposite shoulder, displacing the weight onto your back, some small women probably can, but not everyone. It isn't really comfortable that way, it has to just hang on your shoulder straight down.Only has 1 extra pocket, but it is better designed. Has about a dozen slots for pens, plus a large pocket. There is a flap with snaps to make sure everything stays where it should be, it is a good design.Fabric is not as good, but still feels sturdy. I like that it is SIGNIFICANTLY lighter, and a bit smaller. I wanted a daybag/man purse/"brief case" to carry my laptop and sketch book around with me, to work etc... This one is slightly smaller, lighter, has better secured pockets for knick-knacks

review: *Update May 28 2013*Well I'm returning the shoes. After wearing them everyday since I got them I have found a few fatal flaws. The main reason that I chose to return these came about after two solid days of using them on trails in New Mexico. New Mexico is an incredibly hot yet dry place so I didn't think I'd have to be concerned about my feet sweating a bunch as well as both the shoes and my feet stinking like a garbage dump. I've never had a problem with stinky feet and I normally hike in boots and socks in Texas without issue. These shoes just became overly warm during exercise even in the 56 degree temperatures of the Carlsbad Caverns. I don't want to clear a room every time I take my shoes off.The other fatal flaw was that I kept getting a blister even though these shoes fit very well. The heel of the shoe dug into my Achilles tendon and has caused blister after blister. I gave it a pass on the first blister thinking it was just because the shoes were new but getting one time after time begins to drain your confidence in the shoe. Oddly enough I only got the blister on my left foot and never my right which may indicate a problem with just that shoe.In the end I'm sad to see these shoes go back because they looked so nice and performed really well when they weren't causing blisters or overheating. I even considered powders and sprays but these shoes are only a few weeks old and a shoe shouldn't be this much of a hassle.If Merrell comes out with a better version in the future I'll look back into them but for now I'm off to find something else.*End update*I've only worn "regular" shoes and boots since I was a child and while I've thrown in the occasional flip flop I've never tried a "barefoot" shoe. After seeing a pair of the Five Fingers and discounting them as ugly and stupid I then tried them on and thought they were pretty neat but also knew I'd never spend the money for something that looked so weird. Since I liked them and since my old shoes were nearing the point of no return I started looking at these. I hike a lot and I don't mind running so these seemed to be the match made in heaven, at least according to the reviews, and they looked just like my old shoes.I've had the shoes now for about 2 weeks and the first week was weird and painful. I had no idea how these were supposed to fit and they just didn't feel right. Again I'm comparing them to shoes that have more padding then a mattress but still. My toes touched the front and my heel was being strangled in the back. I thought about returning them for a larger size...until I ran in them.Running in them was a whole new world. I can't describe it other than to say I've never been faster and more comfortable in a shoe. The issue of my toes touching became no existent because my toes splayed out and I was naturally curling them a bit anyway to grip the road. They simply are a great running shoe.I've also done extensive walking in these shoes and I can feel muscles I've never felt before even after great stretching. The walking however prompted a blister on my heel because my feet simply were not adjusted to the shoe. The heel is rather narrow and I was getting a blister in a spot that everyone has experienced and knows is not comfortable. I knew this was going to happen but it meant I couldn't wear the shoes for a day or two.I have yet to hike in them but that is coming and I'm looking forward to it because you can really feel the terrain and get a better grip. From my "testing" I've noticed that only small pebbles seem to cause any discomfort due to the minimalist design.Having worn them without socks the entire time I can say that it is pretty weird but my feet are enjoying the experience. You can feel the wind blow through the shoe and cool your feet which is sort of like being barefoot.Thanks to these shoes I'm going to keep with the "barefoot" shoes and I'm certainly going to stick with Merrell. The "regular shoe" appearance and the quality construction are what made me buy it and the performance is what made me a fan. It gets 4 stars instead of 5 simply because I haven't worn every shoe out there and it's possible that there is a better shoe but I have my doubts.

them on and thought they were pretty neat but also knew id never spend the money for something that looked so weird since i liked them and since my old shoes were nearing the point of no return i started looking at these i hike a lot and i dont mind running so these seemed to be the match made in heaven at least according to the reviews and they looked just like my old shoesive had the shoes now for about weeks and the first week was weird and painful i had no idea how these were supposed to fit and they just didnt feel right again im comparing them to shoes that have more padding then a mattress but still my toes touched the front and my heel was being strangled in the back i thought about returning them for a larger sizeuntil i ran in themrunning in them was a whole new world i cant describe it other than to say ive never been faster and more comfortable in a shoe the issue of my toes touching became no existent because my toes splayed out and i was naturally curling them a bit anyway to grip the road they simply are a great running shoeive also done extensive walking in these shoes and i can feel muscles ive never felt before even after great stretching the walking however prompted a blister on my heel because my feet simply were not adjusted to the shoe the heel is rather narrow and i was getting a blister in a spot that everyone has experienced and knows is not comfortable i knew this was going to happen but it meant i couldnt wear the shoes for a day or twoi have yet to hike in them but that is coming and im looking forward to it because you can really feel the terrain and get a better grip from my testing ive noticed that only small pebbles seem to cause any discomfort due to the minimalist designhaving worn them without socks the entire time i can say that it is pretty weird but my feet are enjoying the experience you can feel the wind blow through the shoe and cool your feet which is sort of like being barefootthanks to these shoes im going to keep with the barefoot shoes and im certainly going to stick with merrell the regular shoe appearance and the quality construction are what made me buy it and the performance is what made me a fan it gets stars instead of simply because i havent worn every shoe out there and its possible that there is a better shoe but i have my doubts

review: This was a watch I dearly wanted to like. It looked like it had it all. A FULL analog display, with the digital display BELOW it, instead of hidden underneath the analog hands for 4 hours every day. Design was nice, simple, neutral colors, and the watch itself is not too large to fit on smaller wrists.Perfection? well... not quite! I don't know how Casio's engineers did it, but it is almost as if they purposefully designed the plastic cover of the digital display to catch nearly all angles of light. Then they recessed the display DEEPLY back into the watch face, so if your angle gets too sharp, you can't read it.... But you also can't read it head-on due to glare! You end up having to play hide-and-go-seek anytime you want to read the digital display, carefully angling your wrist until you find the sweet spot. You end up doing that instead of the simple action of just looking at your wrist, noting the time, and moving on with your day. I returned it after less than two days.
summary: Horrible glare on the digital readout, can't read it! Might as well be analog only!

review: I ordered this necklace for myself after being intrigued with a similar necklace from stella and dot (which cost way too much!).  The necklace is a great value, is beautiful, and a very nice weight, not too heavy or light.  The toggle clasp is very easy to use.  I found my necklace to match the stock photo in color.Just be aware that the necklace is said to be made of "reconstituted turquoise" which is basically turquoise fragments, too small to be used by themselves, which have been powdered and bonded to form a solid mass. review: Useful travel gadget saves embarrassing re-packing at the airport, accurate and works well, but requires a bit of strength to lift and hold your baggage in the air.I've traveled for years, and I'm one of those well-prepared folks who weighs their bags before going to the airport.  But on my return, unless there's a bathroom scale in the hotel (and I'm not running too late), I usually end up having to guess.  Since 2nd bag and overweight bag fees are so high these days, it's important to know your luggage is under the airline's limit.I'd been wanting one of these portable luggage scales for a while, and finally had a chance to try this one out, and used it on a recent trip to Las Vegas.  I found it more accurate than my home method (bathroom scale), and the numbers matched up closely to the airline scales.  It's small and seems sturdy, so easy enough to slip it into a suitcase to take with you after weighing the bag.  My only complaint with the unit is that it's a bit difficult to hold a large bag up in the air in front of you such that you can still read the display- it takes a couple of seconds (or longer if the bag is moving) for the scale to finalize the weight of the bag-- which can feel like a long time when the suitcase weighs 50lbs. I had to use two hands on the scale to support that 50-pounder (which was a bit awkward).The unit looks solid and well-made.  Recommended.

Definitely junior sizing (read: slender/fitted), so order up a size, or two sizes if you don't want clingy. Other reviewers suggested ordering up, so I did. Normally XL, I ordered the 2XL. Still a bit clingy, but OK for gym/casual wear. (I'll be careful to wash cold/line dry to avoid shrinking). Approximate dimensions on 2XL (if this helps):Chest: 39"Waist: 36"Length: 24" (from back)On 5'5" frame, the tee sits right at hips--decent length for overhead exercises at the gym. Great value and I'll be buying more soon if my size is still in stock!
summary: Casual all-cotton for great price, but junior sizing--order UP (at least) one size!

review: I am 5'7", 140lbs, and I bought a size Large US 12 in Black.  The fabric is very soft, almost soft enough to be considered a winter undershirt, or perhaps something to sleep in... except for... the buttons.I'm not sure what to make of these.  There is a line of iridescent, very thin buttons running down the back on a line of folded fabric, ending in a sort of split in the shirt.  It looks similar to a short hospital gown.  It also looks similar to a button-down shirt, but turned backwards, and with false buttons.  Thankfully, there are no other splits in the shirt apart from the one on the bottom of the back, but the bat wing design makes it kind of awkward.If the buttons bother you so much that you are embarrassed to wear the shirt, then it seems that they are only attached by a bit of string, and can be cut off very easily.  Given, you will still have the fabric fold and split on the back, but at least you won't have any shiny buttons.

review: I had been looking for months for board shorts that don't flare out and make me look like a cow. When I found these on Amazon, I actually called the manufacturer to ask them if they're lined, so I wouldn't have to wear anything underneath it (I had previously resorted to wearing spandex workout shorts, which were too thick and which needed underwear). The rep checked and said yes. Well, that wasn't true.There is a tiny "panty" diamond in the nether region, where several seams meet, and that little spot is lined, but that's it. There's no built-in "underwear" or panty to these swim shorts, and the material is so thin (as thin as cheap underwear), I felt almost naked underneath it. It is considerably thinner than standard bathing suit material, so if you had it in a light color, you'd be compromising your modesty (I bought mine in black). I ended up wearing a pair of underwear beneath it the second time I wore it and was instantly more comfortable. But I shouldn't have to do that...it should be fully lined and not quite so thin!The length was good, although the waist band's elastic is a bit too strong. There is a tiny mesh pocket inside that's big enough to hold a house key but not much else, so it's rather pointless.I am keeping them because it is so impossible to find swim shorts that are long enough without being baggy, but I'm not thrilled with them. I guess I'll just be packing extra underwear for all my beach trips!
summary: thin material and it's NOT lined!

review: Being a diver I find nearly 300 pairs of lost sunglasses per summer so have owned and worn nearly every brand, make, and model.  I've worn Oakley, Gucci, Prada, RayBan etc..  They all are about the same in quality although I have seen $300 plastic Gucci frames that were not straight and could not be fully straightened. These rank as high in quality as any of them.  This is the first pair I've found and will be replacing a pair of Oakley as my go to sunglasses. At around $50 this is a steal.  I would expect to pay $130 or more for similar Ray Ban Aviator style sunglasses.  The metal is very high quality - not flimsy at all.  Took me a while to adjust them.  They are stiff.  The lens optics are good although mine had a few light scratches when I found them.  Slide on and off easily but the fit feels secure.  If you are looking for good quality and don't care about the name you should check these out.

After considering other models with colored faces, the black seems easier to read than blue or the lighter colors. The rotating bezel is a feature I wanted for timing dives. The MSRP on Invicta watches is definitely expensive for my wallet but Amazon offers a sensible price. The look and feel of the Invicta 6977 Pro Diver watch is class and quality. If you are considering an Invicta don't let the size and weight scare you away. It's a quick and easy adjustment. review: I have run off and on for 33 years, mostly in the range of a few miles a day.  My goal for a running shoe is comfort, plain and simple.  You will likely hear that you have to spend at least $100 for a good pair of running shoes these days and it is largely true.  Durability is not just a function of tread-wear for me.  A shoe has to maintain the integrity of the midsole and assure heel stabilization to keep your foot in a neutral position upon landing. Here is where this Gel Nimbus failed me.  I am a neutral runner and the shoe does some strange things as it wears.  The gel inserts are only on the outside of the shoe.  In my case, the rear inside sections of the outer sole collapsed into the mid-sole.  The gel on the outside kept that part of the sole stable.  Basically the shoe began to shift inside upon impact, significantly at about 100 miles.  Further, the front section of the sole had worn out at around 125 miles.The Asics Gel Nimbus 14 is too soft, in my opinion.  It needed more gel reinforcement on the inside of the shoe.  The sole wears out too quickly.  It is simply, not worth the money.  The uppers wore very well, so I was left with a good looking shoe that was useless after half the mileage that it should have given me.I would say that I just had a bad pair, but I had bought a second pair before I noticed the problem with the first pair.

review: I have reviewed both the Sanuk Yoga Mat Flip Flop, which I fell in love with last summer, and the Sanuk Yoga Wildlife in Cheetah, which I had to return based on a disagreement between my foot and the cut of the shoe. I am a big fan of Sanuk. It's an eco-conscious company, with a great focus on producing good products while contributing to the community and supporting worthy causes, such as Operation Smile. I mention this because of my preference to buy from companies that pay their workers fairly and do something positive for the world rather than just churn out low-quality products made by cheap labor that pollute the environment.Sanuk made this style last year, but this is the first time I have tried something other than a traditional flip flop from them. I like to slip my shoes on my way out the door and I knew these would require an extra minute or two. I have had surgery on both ankles and they tend to be very sensitive so I was slightly concerned about discomfort at my achilles tendon, based on the stretchy band inside the material.The Sanuk Yoga Sling 2 fit as well I hoped and looked better than I expected. I had seen images of them on, but they were promos for the shoe so the model had perfect feet, long and narrow. My feet are slightly wide and the soles of Yoga Sling 2 looked as though they might be less wide than those of the Yoga Mat. I actually asked this question on Sanuk's site and was disappointed that it was never answered.With Amazon's free return policy on this product, I took a chance and ordered them in the Black/White, size 9. At first I thought I was losing my mind because I seemed to be missing a strap. Turns out the back strap was wrapped underneath each shoe. Once I had them on properly, I was pleased as punch. They look great on my non-modelesque, normal person feet.The material is soft against my skin and between my toes (it's the same material all throughout the shoe). My concerns about the strap around the ankle were unfounded. Though strong enough to easily hold the shoes on, the elastic within the fabric is gentle enough not to cause me any discomfort.The pattern of the Black/White looks exactly the same as it does online. These shoes are casual but can definitely be dressed up depending on your ensemble. They provide more options than a traditional flip flop, while offering the same level of comfort.I was correct about the Yoga Sling 2 requiring an additional minute or two to put on compared to my original Yoga Mat Flip Flops. It's mostly making sure the fabric is laying flat (for looks) and is not a big deal.Like the Sanuk Yoga Mat Flip Flops, the footbed of the Yoga Sling 2 is made from recycled yoga mats and is just as squashy and cushiony. However, the Yoga Sling 2 has what Sanuk calls a "sealed edge", which is just what it sounds like and adds to dressier quality of the shoe. You can clearly see the difference when you zoom in on the images on the product pages.There are 2 slightly negative things about footbed of these shoes, which is why I gave them 4 instead of 5 stars.1) As other reviewers mentioned, the footbed is shiny (rather than matte like the Yoga Mat Flip Flops) and this finishing treatment tends to stick to my feet, especially when my they are sweaty, which is often. I read that one reviewer stuck moleskin on the footbed, which resolved her issue.2) The front of the footbed has a slightly odd shape. It is rounder and more bulbous than I expected and extends a fair amount around the front of my foot. It almost makes the shoe look too long, like it's the wrong size. (I had a similar problem with the Sanuk Yoga Wildlife Flip Flop) I haven't seen anyone else mention this so perhaps mine were cut weird. However, I haven't had this issue with my Yoga Mats. Unlike the problem I had with the Yoga Wildlife Flip Flops, the back of the footbed on the Yoga Sling 2 looks and fits fine. Though I was on the fence about returning them due to the front of the footbed, I like the way they look overall, they are super comfortable, and it's good to have a "dressy" flip flop option, considering that's all I wear most of the year.Overall, the Sanuk Yoga Sling 2 is cute, comfortable, and versatile. Amazon carries all the colors (depending on your size). They also carry the Sanuk Yoga Sling 2 Prints Flip Flop, which I am kinda dying for in the Tomato Tie Dye.

review: OKlet me preface this review by saying that iI have fairly average width smallish feet. I normally wear a 7, but after reading the comments on other reviews noting difficulty in getting feet into these boots, I ordered the 7.5. I have very high arches so I normally wouldn't even attempt boots that didn't have a zipper. The length is actually really good. It was a bit tight getting my feet in but its doable. Its not difficult enough to discourage me from wearing them at all. I have thin heels so I do have wobble room in the back but idk how that could be avoided and me be able to get my foot in there. review: 60% COTTON and 40% POLYESTER T SHIRT. Now, it was described as 100% cotton, and that's what I wanted to buy. If the sellers are not able to honestly describe their product, then they will lose the ability to sell to people via the web. ITS IMPERITIVE that the sellers are honest, for us to buy sight unseen.  People don't realize, perhaps, that polyester cotton blended cloth is much cheaper than pure cotton. PURE COTTON breathes in hot weather, and absorbs sweat better.  The shirt looks OK, the color and size is nice, so is the cut.  BUT, it will wear out MUCH faster, than 100% cotton. It wont fade in that cool way that 100% cotton does, nor will it shrink a bit, which is why I buy 2X for 100% cotton. I'm VERY DISAPPOINTED, especially because my TIME was wasted, simply because the seller felt like being dishonest.

review: I've purchased two of these. NEVER AGAIN. The first had the belt separate from the buckle because the holes in the belt that fit into two small screws that hold the belt to the buckle tore resulting in the belt being no longer attached to the buckle. I was able to disassemble, cut off the torn end, punch new holes and reattach. I chalked it up to s fluke and went ahead and ordered a second because I liked the look, price and fact it was reversible. Well now in less than two years both have had the belts themselves tear. It is easy to see why. The so-called leather belts are in fact two very thin pieces of leather stitched over a central filler core of some material that does not seem to bear any resemblance to leather. The thinness of the outside leather is just not up to normal wear. Save your money and look for something that is more substantial.

review: I purchased this backpack for my son, who is in fourth grade.  He has only been using it for a few weeks, but so far this backpack is holding up well to his daily abuses. It appears to be very well made and highly durable, you can tell by the look and feel of it. The fabric is thick and sturdy, and it is stitched well throughout. There are tons of compartments in this backpack; both internal and external...there is a place for almost anything you can think of that you would carry with you to school (or elsewhere). review: HOW I USE THESE: I like to run, but I haven't tried them for that because I had knee surgery two months ago and am not yet cleared to run. I have worn these for long walks (by myself, with two dogs, and with two kids), physical therapy appointments (including lunges, squats, and heel raises), and cardio machines (elliptical and bike mostly). I have rheumatoid arthritis in addition to the recent knee surgery, so support and comfort are crucial to me, and these definitely met my needs.SIZING: I usually wear an 8 and ordered an 8. They fit perfectly, so they are true to size.COLOR: The picture on my screen was an exact representation of the real-life color. If you look at the picture, you can note that the purple Puma swoosh on the side is a little more of a classic purple color while the base of the shoe is slightly more plum. The lime green areas are BRIGHT, but I like it that way. Unlike the picture, though, the gray Puma embroidered at the toe isn't very obvious, nor is the texture on the gray fabric toward the toe. The texture near the heel is pronounced, though, and looks very nice.SUPPORT/COMFORT: I was worried these might not provide enough support, because the toe box is on the roomy side and because I couldn't feel much to any arch support, which I usually need. However, these are now my go-to sneakers for working out and wearing around town.COMPARISON TO OTHER BRANDS: I've used New Balance, Saucony, and Nike in the past, and these are my favorite. I haven't worn Pumas since middle school, so I can't compare them to other Pumas.

review: (Long review, intended to help those uncertain about the Korean sizing, and other related issues.)I bought this along with the "TheLees Mens casual buckle zipper slim hoodie T-shirts." I thought it would be a good, tight shirt to put underneath it, and my instinct was right. Well, sort of.I must reiterate what another reviewer did: with this shirt in particular, I don't think you should be afraid to order it smaller. It's very elastic and can handle being stretched. I honestly wish I would have gone close to the smallest size. While it's tighter than the other shirt I bought from TheLees I really wanted to have this one hug my body. For you nerds out there, I was going for a similar look to Leon S. Kennedy from Resident Evil 4. (After he lost his jacket.)I'm 5'11 and around 160 pounds, 30 waist, and the XL size was recommended for my height at least. My weight is more close to the Medium or Large size, but I wanted to make sure I could get longer sleeves. Sadly though, I think I would have traded slightly shorter sleeves for a better fit. I'd have to gain over 30 pounds of muscle to make this conform to my body the way I wanted.The chest and arms define reasonably well, but I'd like it tighter. If you don't care about showing off your physique / treating it like a compression shirt, a larger size might be the way to go. Because on the positive side, it's extremely comfortable, and isn't bad looking just because it isn't latching onto my body.I have nothing bad to say about this shirt. It's warm enough on a cloudy, or windy day, but it won't help you much during the Winter. I definitely recommend it regardless of whether it's a tight or loose fit; it looks good no matter what.Don't hesitate on this one. But I do suggest smaller size on this one in particular, given my experience, and what one of the other reviewers said. (He was 25 pounds heavier, 2 inches taller than me and had no problems with the smallest size! Definitely wish I would have gotten at least a size or two smaller than I did.)Anyway, hope this was useful.
summary: Ignore the US sizing on this one.

review: With extra wide flat feet, I have never been able to find a shoe that I could comfortably wear without socks.  No matter what brand I bought I wound up with blisters on the heels, sore toes, or deep trenches dug into the top of my foot.  I have a pair of Sperry's that are fine to wear if I am sitting down, but within ten paces a couple of blisters will already have appeared and make my day miserable.  So for a recent trip to Italy I took a reach and ordered the Saranacs sight unseen based on their reviews.  On my first day in Italy I wore them without socks - and then never wore anything else for the remaining 8 days of my trip, staggering considering I averaged more than 4 miles a day of walking.  They are undoubtedly the most comfortable summer shoes I have ever owned.  Nice padding and support, they seem to breath well, offer enough give to keep them from digging in anywhere, and they look great.  Very versatile too; I wore them with shorts, blue jeans, and chinos and they looked appropriate in all settings.  As for size, I have read that some people feel they run big.  This could be true, as I typically wear a size 10, ordered a size 10 of the Saranacs, and they seem to accommodate the extra width needed for my feet.  Find the size that works for you and you can't go wrong.  I would give them 10 stars if I could.
summary: Maybe the best shoe I have ever owned...seriously

review: Puma Heroes T7 Track Jacket (Red, Medium)The Heroes T7 jacket appears to have a similar fit as Puma's other track jackets, namely the Scuderia Ferrari track jacket I reviewed earlier. I usually wear a size medium track jacket or windbreaker, and all of Adidas' Medium Track Jackets / Windbreakers fit with just enough clearance in the arm pits to raise my arms as well as move comfortably. The Heroes T7 and the Scuderia Ferrari jackets are slightly shorter at the waist and tighter at the arm pits than I'd like. I wear a size medium, and would recommend a size Large if I had to buy another jacket.The material is still rather thin, and I'd say the Heroes T7 is very similar to the Scuderia Ferrari jacket with the exception of the logoes of course, but at more than half the price. The Puma logo is adhered to the polyester jacket, and I wish Puma embroidered the logo. The white stripe and the Puma red go well together, and reminds me of the Puma clothing for Ducati Corse and Scuderia Ferrari. The cuffs at the hands are very comfortable, as is the cuff at the waist. The pockets are a bit stiff and vertical, while the zipper has a huge Puma logo shaped  handle. The construction of the track jacket makes it comfortable for jogging, and more than adequate for casual wear. I do not recommend wearing it below 50 degrees F though, as it is not well insulated.Overall: 4/5 stars, the fit is a tad tighter than competitor's jackets of the same size. Go 1 size larger to be on the safe side.
summary: Awesome Puma styling and design, size is a tad tight

review: I ended up buying this for my wife during her 3rd trimester of pregnancy, as she was afraid of her postpartum belly after the baby would be delivered.  Prior to pregnancy, she was a size 10 in pants (which equates to roughly 31-33 inches around the WAIST, not HIPS).  However during my wife's pregnancy, she had polyhydramnios, which occurs in 1 in 250 pregnancies, around her 2nd trimester of pregnancy and towards the late 2nd trimester, was borderline preeclamptic/pregnancy induced hypertension (she eventually was admitted for preeclamspia).  Polyhydramnios is the excessive amniotic fluid surrounding the unborn infant.  With the preeclampsia, she held onto excessive fluid and both these conditions caused her belly/body to literally explode in size.According to Belly Bandit's sizing notes:*Sizing: Measure the circumference of your belly just prior to delivery, and subtract 3-5 inches. XS: 28-32". SM: 33-37". MED: 38-43". LG: 44-49".Since my wife experienced polyhydramnios & preeclampsia, my wife measured literally in the lower 50s for her belly size prior to delivery.  Using that guide, I ordered a LG (44-49") because ~50s minus 3 to 5 inches would land her in the LG range (per Belly Bandit's instructions). 3 days after she delivered, she started using the LG Belly Bandit.  It barely fit, but she wore it religiously.  Her doc said the symptoms of fluid from preeclampsia would go away in about 2 weeks after delivery.  Around the 1st 2 weeks, she loved it.  It fit borderline snug/tight and really shrank her belly nicely.  But by the 3rd week, this device is no longer useful and now we have to buy another one.  I'll get to that in a minute....PREGNANCY WEIGHT LOSS: Based on our pregnancy class, they reported that the average weight loss from delivery alone is roughly 10 lbs to 12 lbs.  The baby is majority of the weight, followed by 2 lbs of blood/amniotic fluid and 1-2 lbs of the placenta.  In the 1st week, you will lose 3-5 lbs of water weight.To understand how much weight my wife has lost, around the 2nd week, my wife went back in to check w/ her doctor on her overall condition.  The total weight loss from delivery to the 2nd week was about 26 lbs.  Remember, my wife had polyhydramnios and symptoms of preeclampsia.  Meaning MINUS the ~10-12 lbs of pregnancy weight lost, she lost an additional ~15 lbs of fluid/water weight.  This was 3x times more than a normal pregnancy.So that is why this Belly Bandit LG size does not work on my wife any longer.  She can't even use this because the size of her waist has shrunk so much, the belly bandit won't even fasten around her waist (the velcro on one side goes past the entire velcro area on the other side).  So since then, I've had to purchase another one for my wife.I know while not everyone will experience what my wife has experienced, there is a sizing discrepancy since many customers have either bought 2 or more Belly Bandits or complain that the sizing chart is off.  If Belly Bandit were smart, they would EXPAND the size of the area where the velcro attaches to allow for a broader size coverage.  If you look at the sizing chart, they go through 5 inch increments from size to size.  Considering my wife's condition during pregnancy, 5 inches is TOO SMALL of a range to cover her fluid loss.So as you can guess in this review, this item does work, but because of the way it's made, you'll likely need to buy another one during your postpartum weight loss.
summary: *DOES* Work, But You Need to Invest In Two+ Due To Sizing/Shrinkage, EXTRA NOTES IN REVIEW

review: This 22mm silicone band is excellent, certainly the most comfortable band I've ever worn. As soon as I took it out of the package and felt it, I was impressed. It felt soft, very flexible in my hand. I've bought a number of sport watches with rubber or poly straps and this silicone model just leaves the others in the dust.This is a simple, black sport band with no taper. It's thick enough to be sufficiently tough for any purpose and any watch. Obviously, I would not buy this for a dress watch, but for sport watches this is an excellent choice. It comes at a very modest price and is certainly competitive with the best sport straps as far as comfort and price. If you notice in the pictures, it comes with appropriate size pins already in the band!It isn't without a flaw, however. My wrist size is on the smaller end of the spectrum and fits me absolutely perfectly. Usually, watch bands are sized toward larger wrist sizes, but not in this case. If I were a man with a large wrist, I would have to pass on this fine band.For me, this was an excellent purchase. Five stars.Update April 17, 2014: This has been a good strap. It's still on the same watch I originally put it on. It's still soft and flexible. It's still intact and I have not had any problems with the band or the pins that originally came with it. It's been a good band for me, regardless of how much sweat, scrapes or knocks it has had to deal with.

I don't think it detracts much as I really don't need it at night and I will not knock a star off for it. The winding seems smooth and mostly quiet just as my other.I love this like many other Seiko Auto watches and wish they had wider distribution in the states.Quick update: After over 7 months of use I still love this watch. review: The size and layout of this satchel were thoughtfully designed.  It's an attractive and accommodating  bag that works just as it was designed to do.  The double handles can be placed on your shoulder with the pocketbook tucked under the arm and close to the body, or the optional cross-body strap can be employed for completely hands-free placement at hip level, should that be more convenient.  I prefer the look without the long (adjustable) strap, but there are times when I find it convenient.There's one design feature of which you would do well to be aware.  That's the fact that when opening this bag, a single zipper curves upward toward the top, runs straight for a while, and then curves downward.  When a zipper follows a curved line on soft material, it can be easily caught or damaged.  Take your time; no speeding along those curves, and you shouldn't have an accident!

review: I bought this to wear around in the evenings during the cooler months which around here runs from now (October) to April.  I slip out of my bluejeans and put these on.  I wear it practically every evening now.  It is soft and durable.  Had a pair for a year now, wash them regularly in normal wash and it is holding up fine.  The fly is convenient and the two pockets are a must.  After all, you need some place to stick a tissue at the very least.  I am 5'10" with a 32 waist so I ordered medium and it fits good.  Legs are not tight or form fitting (which I would not like for casual in house wear).  I can step outside for a few minutes and take out the garbage or do something and not feel like I am being too riskee' if a neighbor sees me.  Easy to put on and take off.  The fabric tie for the waistband works and the ties are thick and not easily broken.  It looks pretty much just like the photo...dark navy blue and black flannel weave.  Perfect for when your house temperature is lower during colder periods but would probably feel too warm on a summer's night when the house is warmer.  I don't wear these to bed, heck, I don't wear anything to bed so I cannot comment on comfort for sleeping.  I liked the first pair so much I just ordered a second one so I can rotate them during the week.

review: I bought the 2 pack in grey with navy block lettering at Burlington.  There were only two packs and I wish I bought the other one.  I returned to buy it but it was gone.  Not many people are into luxury designer underwear so maybe they just put it in the stockroom.The fabric is the best part.  It is about 96% cotton 4% elastic.  It is loose yet clingy on thighs it does not flare open like its stretched out.  It more or less moves with you without compressing or being tight.  The fit is really good and I do not think it will lose it's shape.  That happened to me with the Hilfigers.The block lettering looks way better and more heterosexual.  I like boxer briefs with big logos on thick waist bands to wear with my jeans or shorts kinda like 50 cent or ll cool j.  I like the sporty athletic look.  Working on abs and chest so body looks good with low rise jeans and underwear showing when I lift my shirt.  I am fashionable in the urban sense.  I wear a lot of nike and loose jeans fitted at the waist.  I do not like tight jeans or no show underwear.  I like to show off.  Having low rise jeans with thick waist band logos and tight abs is hot like shaun t.  I like that hiphop abs look. The fancy distressed look is to ambiguous for me.  I don't want to give off bisexual vibes so would never purchase the seven7 boxers with the font that amazon is currently offering.  Just too tacky.  They have it in weird colors like magenta and purple pink or teal with a animal print like distressed lettering.  In all it looks pretty bad.  I bought my pair to look good.  These do not so it would not be worth the money.Most guys fashion is based around function rather than aesthetics.  Men who dress too fancy are often seem as effeminate.  These are the more effeminate boxers that seven7 offers which is disappointing because they have ones that are much much better.I bought these as a cheap alternative to the CK steel I usually wear.  I work out a lot now and wanted something for the gym.  The CK steel trunks are comfortable and I sometimes wear them but they are simply to expensive for the demanding workouts that I do.  I want to keep the CK nice for a while instead of wearing them out so the seven7 help me out with that.Both the Calvin Klein and the seven7 boxer's are better than the Tommy Hilfiger.  The Tommy Hilfiger loses it's shape on the legs.  The seven stay's in place and is comfortable and is longer length then the Calvin Klein trunks I have so it seems more appropriate for the gym.  I could have gotten the Adidas boxers for working out but the seven7 just looker better and I can wear them for casual use as well.  The nike pro combat was way too tight and seems I would have to go up a size.  When things have proprietary fits instead of standardized sizing that's just weird to me.  Most men's sizes tend to run true to standards.  Women/unisex/homosexual styles are usually the ones into vanity sizing.  I really hate wasting money on clothes that don't fit because they lied about size.  It's like a scam to make you buy two or three of the same thing just to get your size right.  Sometimes it's more about the customer and what the market wants instead of a designer who thinks he's all that telling everybody how to wear his fashions or else he doesn't want you wearing them.  I understand how clothing is a representation of one's lifestyle, culture, personality, self expression, and identity but sometimes clothes are just clothes and you have to wear what looks good on you not what's in style or someone says you are supposed to because of presumed reigning stereotypical trends.If I can find more seven7 boxers like the ones I have now I would buy more, but I would never buy these as they are just too ugly and obviously second best.  I would give mine 5 stars for fit, comfort, style, price, durability, and overall value.  I would give these 3 stars because I do not like the look.

review: These gloves are so classy yet you can wear them with anything at anytime. And they are very warm. Suede gloves can be an excellent alternative to leather and other types of fabrics, I purchased these in black, size small but when I put them on they were kind of tight so I had to stretch them out a little by putting them in a small sturdy plastic bag with a secure zipper top with water. Placed the plastic bag into each glove. Placed the gloves, with the bag of water inside, into a larger Ziploc bag and placed the entire thing in the freezer. Let them freeze overnight. Remove the gloves from the freezer and take them out of the larger plastic bags. Allow the plastic bag inside the gloves to thaw out, then remove it. Watch the bag closely as it is melting. If it appears to be leaking, remove the bags at once. Put the gloves on. They should now be stretched so that your hands fit into them more easily. With your hands in the gloves, you can manually stretch the fingers to make them more comfortable. Then wear them on the coldest day and you will see how great they look and feel. I received several compliments on these gloves....LOVE THEM!!

review: Out of the box, this Invicta #10917 Pro Diver Watch definitely leaves quite an impression. With its 43mm stainless steel casing and large black face, this watch is not going to be overlooked. Like other Invicta watches that I own, its not for the wimpy wrists. After six weeks of continuous wear, these are my thoughts.The number one thing that sold me on this watch is the overall styling. Unlike some of the wimpy watches in the jewelry stores, its 100% man watch. Like other Invicta watches that I own, the luminous feature is hit and miss. Its certainly not one of this watch's strongest points. This model also sports an easy to see second hand. Encased in this stealthy timepiece is Swiss movement. The bezel is unidirectional. The side edging offers a solid refined finish.Unlike my other Invicta Dive series watches that are outfitted with heavier bands, the polyurethane band on this watch is much lighter and thinner. Even though this watch is advertised as water resistant up to the depth of 330 feet, its not a serious tool for divers. The folks at Invicta definitely get a standing ovation for the new magnified bubble date window on this watch. I hope that this feature becomes the standard on future additions to the Invicta line.This Invicta #10917 Pro Diver Watch comes nicely packaged with an impact resistant case. The exterior is yellow and branded with a large Invicta logo on the top. The interior is black slotted foam with cutouts for the placement of three watches. The foam is also removable. This case sports two lock slots and comes equipped with a pressure equalization valve. The double throw latches are way better then I expected. To be honest, I wasn't all that sold on the water proof hype until I tested it. The seal did hold its own with zero leaks.This Invicta watch also includes a one year manufacturer's warranty, a five year extended warranty application and product ID card. Florida based Invicta sports an above average website with customer support numbers and email links. A repair pricing chart and shipping charges are also posted.Even though its smaller and definitely lighter then my other Invicta watches and lacks a long lasting luminous feature, its a keeper. This model brings it home nicely.

review: Even my boyfriend liked these shoes and he doesn't like anything I buy!! lol  Seriously, these are really cute on your feet, very stylish and "in" right now and super comfortable.  Ladies - you HAVE to cut the plastic off the thong for maximum comfort. I've been wearing thongs for so long (lifetime Cali girl) that the plastic doesn't really bother me. However, I know it's not meant to be worn that way as I've had many pairs that come with this plastic. It's a little tough to cut off, but once you do your feet will feel great in these. Nice padded insole, cushioned outer sole with just the tiniest bit of a heel, and a true foot shaped foot bed make these a good buy. So, the "Black" is more of a dark grey/burnt olive looking color, has that worn look... perhaps not the best sandal for dressier outfits... and maybe not a great shoe for anyone with wide feet. review: The Invicta 9094 pro diver watch looks like $5,000 of watch hanging on my wrist. It's nothing short of stunning in saphire blue.It's a tough watch and good for people who have outdoor jobs or peform dynamic tasks like engineering test work.The stainless steel bracelet gleams and the watch keeps good time. The bezel also turns easily enough. It is not a "coin edge" like Rolex uses, but "scalloped" like an Omega. That means the wearer cannot turn it from the side like a radio knob. You have to use your thumbs and press down slightly to move it like a wheel, which, of course, it is. There have been some complaints by other reviewers about the Invicta bezels not turning well, but the three Invicta dive watches I've purchased turn with ease after a little use and practice.To recap: Absolutely stunning looking watch at an affordable price. People will notice it and it works well as a tough watch to stand up in extreme environments.

review: I have been a boot addict for years. I tend to go back to brands I know and love. Although I had been looking at DV for quite awhile, - a decrease in the price pushed  me to take the plunge and 1 click the sale. The description and feedback were my biggest reason for purchasing these boots, (especially with my limited knowledge on the brand). I am 5' 4.5" and weigh about 143 pounds. This is short and  thin enough to find the boot width and height very important.  My calves would be considered under 15" circumference and after many disappointing online purchases of other brands, these DV boots are perfect!  They come directly under my knee but the V cut makes them very comfortable. I wear an 8.5 M and these are true to size.  The soles are also leather and quite slick, but, after the first wear, and many swirls on concrete, not dangerous any longer. I love the aged look. As far as pulling them on and off- they go on easily and I usually can take them off with little effort. After a month of wear, the boots are holding their shape! There is no slouching happening with these. They can be worn with skinny jeans and leggings or under boot cut jeans. They were a hit at the rodeo among more traditional western boot wear.  DV has hit the mark  and exceeded my expectations with these!  Five stars all around on this purchase.

review: I bought the black and blue versions of this fine watch. They serve as my day-to-day watch. As noted in other reviews, a self-winding watch is more accurate than a mechanical watch but far less accurate than a quartz watch. Quartz watches are off by about 2-3 seconds/month, compared to atomic clocks. Self-winding watches are off by about 7 minutes/month. I've found the difference to be negative not positive. Sieko says about 5 minutes/month.But, here's one advantage: you won't have to wind it, just wear it. That makes it the best watch available in the future ahead. It's getting harder to get my battery replaced on quartz watches and it will likely become impossible in future.It only takes about 39 hours for the battery to run down so it helps to rotate it (to wind) about 2-3 minutes a day. I can usually wind it in a commercial break.The drawbacks with the black model:* harder to keep clean;* the day and date diasl are harder to read because the face is black and so are the letters.All in all, it's a great watch.If this review was helpful, please add your vote.____________________________________________________________My review of the blue watch:Xbar 107 seconds/week (runs fast) (7 min./month compared to 2-4 sec./month quart watch)St DEV 27 sec.N = 795% CI 2.447 using Student's tm+ = 136 seconds/weekm- = 78 seconds/weekSummary:Good points:* Beautiful with dark blue face, white symbols, functional medium blue nylon band;* Easy to read, even in the dark;* Easy to use with uncomplicated settings similar to other watches;* Compact design;* Measurement consistency is excellent compared to quartz mechanism.Bad points:* Difficult to set time (see instruction below);* Gains 10 seconds a day (consistency); and* Does not have a manual wind stem.DiscussionThis watch looks great. Unlike other self-winding or kinetic watches, this one is compact. In the best tradition of Japanese product development they thought hard on how this watch should function and say about the wearer. The hands are easy to reading, even for those of use with failing eyesight. The second sweep hand has a red tip allowing easy setting of the time.Unlike some Japanese product, they provided a short, simple instruction on winding the watch. Here, I am comparing them to Citizen's ecco-drive watch, which I also own. Just gently work your arm back and forth for three minutes and the self-wind watch will charge; or, you could wear it for a day.Okay, now for the negatives. First, this is not your quartz watch. The original self-winding mechanism was invented, according to Wikipedia, by a Swiss watchmaker in 1770. Since then it has gone through several improvements but is not as accurate as the quartz mechanism popularized starting in the 1970's. Sieko warns that this watch could be off by as much as 10 seconds a day. One days use indicates that my watch runs about 10 seconds fast.The great watchmaker Harrison, who invented the first chronometer accurate enough to track longitude, said that consistency is more important than accuracy. (A poor copy of one of Harrison's chronometers was carried by Captain Cook on his circumnavigation of the globe. He had nothing but good things to say about it.) I have another self-winding watch, an Armitron, which runs consistently 15 seconds fast on days that I wear it.From my measurements of quartz watches, their accuracy, while dazzling when compared to old hand-wound watches, are less accurate than my old German pendulum clock. So I am told, the longer the arm, the more stable the swing and the more accurate the timepiece. I compare everything to atomic time. And, because the quartz mechanism is battery-driven, it seems to suffer from inconsistency. I carefully measured my collection of six quartz watches over a 2 year period. While the battery was new, the error was consistent. As the battery burned out, the error became difficult to predict. Sometimes a positive error (adding time) would become a negative error (losing time) as the battery died. This dying period was also hard to predict.The accuracy of the quartz mechanism is not as good as my pendulum clock. They all lost or gained approximately 2-3 seconds, on average, per month.Based on a week of measurements with the Armitron, I would say that it is shockingly inaccurate but consistently so. Assuming that the Sieko error continues to be consistently 10 seconds fast this will mean that I will have to reset it every week. This is a small price to pay for an otherwise beautifully crafted and honestly-priced handiwork.Setting timeThe date and day are fairly easy. I suggest turning the watch arms until the click past a day so you won't be twelve hours off.Setting the time is another matter. What works best is to set the hour and minute hands, then gently hold the second hand. It should pulse a little as it tries to force itself forward. Once the seconds are accurate, release and the second hand should be accurate. It takes a little practice at first and don't set the time until you pump the watch 3 minutes to charge the spring.Time to unwindIt takes about 39 hours (1 test) to unwind.

review: I bought two of these hats for I like the looks of them, and the fact they were listed as oversized. I must say now that I have received them they are both very nice looking, and as stated well oversized in my opinion. I don't really have a fat head, (not to be confused with being a fathead), but I do like winter hats like this to fit loosely. They are just a lot more comfortable and better looking on your head in my opinion. This hat is made of quality material, thick and warm, and I really like the looks of the ribbed design in it. I don't think too many people would be disappointed in the looks and quality of it...That being said, I have to say I was still somewhat disappointed in the lack of real slouch in it. Even though it fits loose and comfy on my head it still doesn't slouch as much as it does in the picture. The reason why all these hats look slouchy in the pictures is because no one is wearing them. Once stretched over the head they stick up and barely slouch at all. It does slouch a little, but just a smidgen in my opinion. And I feel anyone that is looking at this for just that reason alone may be disappointed.In conclusion, this a very nice looking quality hat that is extremely comfortable when wearing it. And if you like the looks of it and are willing to be satisfied with just a tiny bit of slouch, you will probably like this hat very much. For what its worth, I have ordered several of these slouchy hats and have still never found one that truly slouches as advertised. But this one is the best of the lot, and by far the nicest looking and I am satisfied with it... : )

review: Was expecting this watch to lose/gain 10 seconds per day, as is typical foran automatic watch, but it didn't gain more than 2-3 seconds over a FULLWEEK of careful monitoring!  Very pleased with the accuracy!  The 21 jewelsmust have performed their magic.The band is a bit too narrow at 18mm, and that's really what kept thiswatch from getting a 5 star review.  Soon after receiving the watch, Iordered a beautiful 18mm leather band from "F and E Buckles and Bands".I'll post a photo with the leather band in the product photos above soon.This watch comes in about 25 varieties and colors, so look into each ofthem before deciding on a favorite.  This particular model comes in 4-5colors.The see-through bottom was a nice touch, and you can see the jewels (well,one anyway) inside, as well as the inner workings.It winds smoothly when you move the watch side-to-side, or while wearing.The crown allows setting date and day (1st click), and the time (2nd click)and the watch cannot be wound via the crown, only by moving it side-to-sideor wearing.In general, I would suggest skipping the date on an automatic watch, as it'sa small hassle to keep setting the date when the watch powers down, or at monthend, while making sure you're setting 12noon or 12 midnight.  Much simpler tohave only the time.  I have a quartz watch with a perpetual calendar, and it'ssuch a treat to know the date is always correct.  But this watch is fun, wartsand all.A tip when setting/changing the day by one day: roll the time around the dialuntil the time passes midnight, so the date changes on its own, to ensure you'renot 12 hours off.

review: This is an extremely nice, comfortable and useful money belt.  I travel a lot, both in the United States and out.  To be quite frank, I am more uneasy in an American city than I am in other countries as far as being robbed, but when traveling to other destinations, you have so much more to loose; e.g.  your passport, visas, etc.  You can get in a real mess without these things so why take a chance.This belt fits perfectly.  I should say it fit perfectly once I did a bit of cutting on the strap.  I have a very thin waist and the thing almost could have circled me twice.  This was really no problem though and a quick flick of a knife did the job.The silk material is quite comfortable and the belt is quite safe when wet.  I have been caught out in many a rain storm with mine on and I have never had a problem with leakage.  I also tend to sweat like a pig in certain climates, there again, no problem.Now keep in mind that this thing should not be used like a fanny pack as it was not built for that.  Money, a few travelers checks and papers and that Is it.  You cannot stuff a water bottle, medications, camping gear and a portable toilet in the thing.I was very impressed with the quality and the price of this belt and do recommend it highly.

review: This watch is great for its price. The automatic movement is great. It has shown good accuracy, can keep running overnight, and has lovely sweeping motion. The watch face is very classic, with just enough shine from the 5 logo.There are, however, some issues that explain why it's affordable:- the strap isn't serious. But replacement straps are cheap.- the calendar is sloooooow to turn. While the day dial advances quickly around 11pm, the day of week takes 4 - yes four - hours to turn! 2 to go to Spanish, another 2 to go to the right English day. This is a bit crazy. In fact the manual tells you to avoid adjusting your watch during those hours. My other automatics turn instantly.- the watch knob has not heft. May sound silly, but adjusting the watch (done regularly since automatics are bound to stop) is not a satisfying experience.- similarly the watch frame looks plasticky. It says steel, but it looks plastic. Also very light.- the glass is not sapphire. Not a bit deal, but cheaper.I think this is an amazing watch for its price, and I love the look. In fact with a brown leather strap I've been wearing it daily. That said, it does not feel more expensive than it costs, either, so not a bargain in that sense.

review: I got the CD-2800 with the cleaning cloth and gem cleaner combo. The cleaner has a plastic basket inside to hold small items. Set-up and use is amazingly easy. Before first use, I rinsed the inside (which is made of stainless steel) and the basket.Cleaning your items is as simple as adding tap water, putting your item in and pressing the on button. After that, the cleaner just buzzes for about 3 minutes, then it's done.I experimented with this quite a bit and I found the best combination is warm water with a few drops of regular washing detergent. I had varying success with eye glasses. Grime that was pretty recent was easy to dislodge but grime that was years old from old glasses was difficult to get rid of. This is understandable because the cleaning is done by microscopic air bubbles and that kind of grime needs a metal scraper to scrape off. However, by far we got the best results with gold jewelry. Gold that was dull became bright and shiny. That was a resounding success and the gold items seemed brand new.This cleaner can basically be used for any small objects such as jewelry, watch bands, coins, even dentures, toothbrushes and shaver heads.I think this is a very useful household item and it's at a very decent price. Sample was received for review purposes but it doesn't change my opinion of it.

review: This is my second Seiko 5. I bought the first one in 1990 and would probably still wear it if the band wasn't so tight now... review: Had I paid more attention I probably would have skipped this selection.  All the details about the way these shoes work sound too good to be true.  And, when I opened the CD that gave a full explanation of the shoes I began to worry.  But, I put them on and went for a walk.  Honestly, the first few steps were a little strange feeling.  After that, I was on my way.  At 64, I was concerned about the rocker motion.  If anything it has enhanced my balance.The shoes are a perfect fit.  The material seems to be of high quality.  Most important, these shoes are comfortable right out of the box.My first outing in these walking shoes was my typical 5+ mile morning walk.  I didn't take time to break them in, but there was no need.  The terrain through our neighborhood is definitely hilly.  Ordinarily, my left foot begins to ache about half way the walk.  I injured a couple of toes a year ago.  My toes never ached.  I am now convinced it has something to do with the bottom structure.  There is never any pressure on my toes that often comes with "flat bottom" shoes.After my first couple of walks I did notice that my legs were not tired, but felt more as if I had been engaged in an "in gym" workout.  I don't notice it so much any more, but as I walk I can tell that my calves are being exercised.I'm no expert on shoes, but I can honestly say that I will look for this brand from this day forward. review: I love Orthaheel shoes.  I have two pairs of slippers, a pair of Mary Janes and 2 pairs of sandals (these would be the third pair of sandals - 2 for work/church, 1 casual).  These are lovely and I buy them b/c I have terrible Plantar Fasciitis and these enable me to walk without pain.  However, they run very flat across the top.  I have a "puffy" foot and the straps area ALWAYS tight across the top.  These are no exception.  I'm wearing them despite a bit of discomfort b/c I'm hoping they will stretch out.  My Mary Janes were also very tight across the top (can one have a muffin top foot???) but they eventually stretched out and are now some of my favorite shoes.  They have worn VERY well. OH - I have between a medium to wide width and my toes/sides of my feet don't hang off the sides (it's close on these).  I have a hard time buying flipflops for that very reason and these are okay. I also have short toes and I don't have miles of shoe at the top leftover and the arches and heels fit just right.  I would like it if the footbed was a bit wider, but all in all, they work great and are lovely.  The gems are stitched on.  Totally worth the cost to walk without pain.  I'll update if they don't stretch out and continue to be a bit tight.

review: The boot is an awesome boot.  Beautiful leather.  Loved the buckles around the ankles. First, they ran about one size too small -- meaning they were too tight and snug and uncomfortable in the toe box. Not sleek and conforming to the shape of a woman's ankle (like the Enzo angiolini Shobi boot!).  I still like the boot and am contemplating keeping the size 9 1/2 which I subsequently ordered, despite the "bunchy" ankle issue.  I wear orthotics, and my orthotics fit perfectly in the size 9 1/2!

review: I can't stand it when reviewers say a colour was great or horrible or inaccurate or whatever and don't tell you WHICH COLOUR they are referring to, so I will start by saying that Pumpkin is exactly as pictured. It is a nice burnt orange - not too loud. But I made the mistake of ordering a Large. Boy, is it large! I usually wear 18 or XL depending on the brand, but Roamans is huge. I loved the top and exchanging isn't an option for an international buyer, but as reviewers have said, the larger the size, the bigger the neck and so it reveals WAY too much skin and even falls off the shoulder. I am going to elasticize the neck. I liked the "trapeze" A-line style and the fabric, which is initially stiff, softened up beautifully after one washing, so I decided to get more in Sage and Lime (also exactly as pictured) in Medium. Still over-large, but I will take them in. The puff sleeves are so pretty and I love the shirring at the neck. Love to get more, but three is enough. :) I am delighted to be able to write a review for this top (onestopplus always gives me an "error" message when I try to post there).Just a note: It is very disappointing that the sites which sell this line internationally don't let you post reviews. When you can't try something on in a store but are relying on other customer input to make a decision about fabric, colour, size, etc, reviews are crucial. International duties and shipping are prohibitively expensive, and that means I have to shop online very carefully. This company does not provide much information and their sizing is so off that it means you HAVE to buy several sizes at once to get the right one. Kudos to Amazon for providing an authentic place to share reviews!

review: I need 100% cotton panties, but finding something low rise that did not show above my pants has proven nearly impossible.  I have tried Hanes and other Jockey bikinis, and found them NOT low rise even when they claimed to be.  Elance in the black color has been the lowest rise of all and works ok for both jeans and working out.  They are not as sexy as I had hoped for, but at this point, I'll take what ever I can get.  They are comfortable, true to size and of good quality.This was originally a 4 star review.  And the BLACK color I ordered still is, see above.  However, I also ordered the pink/ivory/peach color combination because I loved the black so much.  I was disappointed.  I ordered the same size, and the package and tags say bikini, but they do not fit like the same pantie.  They are baggy and the rise is much higher than the black, by 1.5 inches (AFTER washing in hot water and drying on high temp).  These are not low rise as the description and box claim.  These panties show above my MID rise jeans (when standing, I do not have to sit for them to show), and I don't mean they "peak".  At least 3/4 of an inch shows in the back.  In my opinion, these fit like high cut briefs, not low rise bikinis.  I have tried so many styles to try to find LOW rise, 100%  cotton underwear.  I thought I had found something close enough to work in the black.  This other color just doesn't cut it for me, and I am now hesitant to order more of the black.  Will they fit like the first set I bought?  Or will it be more wasted money?  I will keep this order to wear while working out.  They won't show under my karate uniform, but I have yet again wasted money one something that does not live up to its description or my expectations.  I don't think expecting consistency between colors is too much to ask.  I mean come on, a 1.5 inch difference in the rise?UPDATE: I have since ordered a package of white and a package of gray shades. The package of white was, again, larger and higher in the rise, not as bad as before, only about 0.5 inch difference, but enough that the fabric bunched and wrinkled because the fit was too large. The gray shades were perfect fit per the size chart, just like the black. Both sold and shipped by Jockey. Don't know why the lighter colors would be larger, but I'm going to stick with ordering the darker colors.
