review: I was afraid to order these online because I worried about them not fitting. I must have measured my feet on 5 occasions based on the directions on the Vibram Fivefingers website. I got the same result every time. When they arrived, I thought I'd never get them on. Since they cover the entire foot and wrap around the base of your ankle, it was quite a challenge. I actually was sweating like crazy and very frustrated. Once I got them on, I was in love. They are so comfy. The next time I put them on, I put some lotion on the heal of my foot.....this makes them go on quick and easy. The instructions on Vibram site for measuring for size worked out perfect for me.Before buying these, I owned a pair of Fila Skeletoes 2.0. I love the Filas also. However, the Vibrams are much more attractive. The Vibrams feel like the same thickness when I am just walking around the house, but I noticed the difference when running. With the Vibrams, I can feel rocks and cracks on the ground. It is more of a "barefoot" running experience. I am not sure if I like the feel of running in my Filas or Vibrams better, but I wanted to note the difference.I have a painful bunion on one foot. This makes running in ordinary running shoes extemely painful. There is no pain at all for me in these. There was no "breaking in" period with these shoes. I went for a run the first time in them (2 days after they arrived) and I got no rubbing or blisters on my feet. I wear them without socks. The toe area of my foot is quite wide and the back of my foot is average to narrow. I worried that the shoes may be too tight in the toe area, but they fit fine. The fabric stretches. These are amazing.
summary: I LOVE THESE!!!!!!!! (use some lotion when you put them on)

review: I hesitated about requesting this time for review, because I already have many, many Timbuk2 bags, most of them custom-made. Where would I put another one? But I also felt qualified to review this, and... I admit it, I couldn't resist.Overall, this is a fantastic bag. It looks very sophisticated, it's easy to carry, holds a ton, and there have been a lot of nice improvements over previous models. There are slightly fewer pockets, but that's actually better, as it as starting to get ridiculous trying to remember where I put something in one of 15 tiny identical slots. The clasps now tuck under the flap, which looks better. The hardware is a little more lightweight than my custom bags, but for me that's fine (possibly better) because I don't really use them that hard. Finally, the strap pad is perfect, while the custom made strap pads are a little stiff and squared.My only complaint is that, like all Timbuk2 messenger bags, they open with a loud velcro noise. For a little while Timbuk2 was including "silencers" so you could cover up one side and prevent the noise, but now you have to buy them separately--and Amazon doesn't sell them. So, as long as you're willing to make a little noise on opening, or you can put in an order to Timbuk2 direct, you're good. Get this bag.

review: Man I was really looking forward to getting this shoe!  I tried the Triumph before this and felt it was too tall and so didn't really flatter my legs.  I was looking for a shorter combat boot, and I liked that this one not only was shorter than its cousin, the Triumph, but that it could also fold down.I opened the box and was delighted!  The color of the boot (I ordered the brown with the blue floral inner design) was just as pictured.  But then it all went downhill once I tried it on.  I loosened the laces and slipped on the boot, and I immediately noticed a feeling as if the laces were cutting into the top of my feet.  Hmm.  Well, I thought, maybe it's just the way the shoe is laced.  So I unlaced the weave and did up the ribbon again, this time in a different format.  Still a cutting feeling from the laces.  I tried again, and again, but each time the laces cut into my foot.  I even tried with the other lace provided, a more classic shoe lace, and that still didn't help.  It's as if the tongue of the shoe isn't thick enough to act as a barrier between the ribbon and the top of the foot.Oh well, I guess that's strike two for Dr. Martens.  Good thing there was free shipping and returns… :(
summary: Color is just as pictured, but that's it

review: I have had this watch for about 10 years now and was just checking around to see if it is still made, and to my surprise it still is, which is good.  I have worn this watch to work almost everyday on from when I got to about 2006.  I stopped wearing it because my cell phone has a clock and was using that.  My wife took over the watch and started wearing it to work.  Her job involved putting her hands in water and chicken fat daily throughout the day.  I thought well that's it for my Swiss Army watch.  Needless to say about 6 months ago I took on a job where I needed a wrist watch.  I found my old watch, the leather band I had put on it was destroyed and the watch was not working.  I brought to my local watch guy and told him to replace the battery.  At first with the new battery nothing happened, watch didn't work.  I told him, the contacts are probably dirty, the watch has been through hell. He took the battery out cleaned the contacts and put the battery back in. Voila, the watch is keeping perfect time!  While I was there I purchased a new band and he cleaned the watch up for me with whatever they use.  With the new band and the cleanup, the watch looks brand new again.  He only charged for the battery, $5, and a new black leather band, $18.  You cannot kill this watch. If this one every really broke, I would not hesitate to buy another in a heartbeat.

review: This bag was a surprise.  I ordered it because I liked the bright turquoise color shown.  This was not the color I received.  It turned up as a pale robin's egg blue. It has silver hardware and the black and white zebra vinyl.  The zipper is on substantial vinyl pieces that actually make a complete closure of the top of the bag.  A snap strap can pull in the two ends to change the shape of the bag.  Each corner has an inside snap to change it from a tote to a smaller bag. Inside the lining is a bright light gold.  This lining has a vinyl feel to it.  Should be cleanable.  Another surprise was the pocket layout inside.  One zippered pocket inside on back wall, another zippered pocket in front of that pocket but still on the back wall.  (This left the center of the bag totally open rather than being a pocket divider down the middle- What a great idea!)  Two open pockets for phone, etc. on front wall.  Pockets trimmed in blue piping. Handles are attached with four silver removable keepers.  Very nice white stitching throughtout.  Outside back has another zippered pocket big enough for keys or cell phone. This bag is medium sized.  It is not large. It didn't come with the heartshaped fob.  That was a disappointment.  They should add this or remove from picture.Later Note:  I took this bag on a four day vacation and some of the white has already worn off the outside of the bag.  I am very disappointed since the bag is brand new.

review: IF you can find this costume in a large, GRAB IT!  I can't figure out why all the size large are gone in this costume and have come to realize that it's the favortie of all the 8 and 9-year-old boys.  It does run small, so if you're buying it for an older child, it probably won't work.  I bought it for a boy who weighs 72 pounds and well, it's "right there" on the size.  If he grows a millimeter, it won't fit anymore.  Don't pay an outrageous price for it either.  The good old local walmart had this same deluxe suit for twenty bucks!  Not in the large, however.  Anyway, my concern with this suit is that the fasteners in the back that hold it together are very small, flimsy velcro that doesn't stay fastened, no matter how hard you try.  For this much money, I think they could have used an entire piece of Velcro, really now!  And, it snags rather easily, so be careful about that.  Other than those two things, this outfit is incredibly cool beyond measure.  It's been the house favorite so far, and all the boys in the neighborhood now are running around trying to get one!  It's also not very warm, is form fitting so it's going to be difficult getting a pair of sweats under this costume.  Highly recommend!

review: Shoes are just as pretty in person. They are very cushy and comfortable and fit my wide feet well. My only complaint was my difficulty in getting the right size. I ordered the kids size to save money - I normally wear a women's 7.5.I own another pair of Asics kids in a 5.5 that fit PERFECT, but the reviews for this model in women's said they run narrow so I ordered a 6. The 6 arrived and felt more roomy than I typically like. I ordered a 5.5 as an exchange, but waited to send back the 6 so I could compare.The 5.5 were too tight in the toebox to the point where it was uncomfortable just walking around the house. I sent the 5.5 back and have been wearing the 6 to work out in, although I still feel like there is just a little too much slippage when I walk/run - I plan to remedy this with insoles. In summation, be careful because there is a significant difference between sizes. If you can afford to order two just to compare and return one, I would do it.

review: The first Cesenia coat I bought was a bit too big; it fit more like a Misses XS than a Petite XS so I returned it (returns are a very smooth process with Amazon which is awesome).  I recently bought the Via Spiga Sondria coat in Black and am so impressed with it and the brand that I hoped another XS Cesenia would fit right and I’m so glad I ordered another because the coat I received today is perfect!!  I bought it in Java, a gorgeous chocolate color that displays a beautiful copper sheen as I move around.  It has the non-removable hood I adore (no unsightly bulky collar that never lays flat), the size of the hood is perfect and the warm, soft fabric lining keeps it from sliding off.  The fox fur trim is thick and luxurious and I love that it’s removable so the entire coat can be laundered (sending just a hood to the cleaners annoys me and most down products can’t be dry cleaned).  I agree with a previous reviewer that the coat looks great without the belt as it’s form fitting and figure flattering.  The streamlined zippered pockets keep my gloves safe and are roomy enough so my gloves don’t make the pockets bulge.  The shorter length still keeps my thighs warm and looks amazing with skinny jeans and riding boots, and the classic style compliments dressier clothing as well.  I’ve received many admiring stares and compliments on both of my breathtakingly beautiful coats.  I happily tell everyone that thanks to Amazon I just bought two fabulous down coats with real fox trim for less than the price of one down coat everywhere else.

review: These headbands came in a three pack and two of the three were GREAT. I have never been able to wear a headband for any length of time because of the headache I would get from the pressure on my temples. These work differently. Instead of putting pressure on your temple the specially designed band works by very lightly hugging the contours of your skull. That's what the curled ends are for. I was able to wear the darker head bands all day with no headache. The only reason I didn't give this product 5 stars is that there is a difference among the bands depending on the color. The blonde one felt uncomfortable the moment I put it on. (The materials that are used to color plastics can affect the properties of the plastic.) My hair is a dark blond so the others work fine.

review: These are really large diamonds first off. The backs are great as well. I bought these for my daughter, and she doesn't know the difference, so she was thrilled.Now to the diamond quality:The diamonds are moderate-heavily included with deposits, very visible (which is very common in earrings btw). So, if you're a man giving these to your wife or girlfriend who is remotely familiar with diamonds, don't do it.FYI: Cloudy is different than being included with deposits or what they call "feathering". Inclusions change the refraction of light, and thus the diamond cannot sparkle thoroughly, but can still be brilliant if the cut is ideal and the color is clear.~ A diamond with a lot of inclusions can be colorless, and they are not a lot less expensive in some cases.~ However, a cloudy diamond can be a fraction of the cost since they are pointless. Because if they are cloudy, then it doesn't matter the cut or the amount of inclusions...either way, it just won't have the brilliance. Those are the type of diamonds you find at chain retail stores for cheap. A cloudy diamond is useless IMO.Colorless diamonds are sought after and incredibly expensive as you may know. But, if you've ever seen one in person compared to a mediocre diamond, then you'll understand why. You can buy a 1/2 carat clear diamond, and it'll look bigger and more brilliant than the mediocre 1 carat..which is why they cost so much more. I have learned my lesson on that years ago. It's always better to go quality over quantity in the case of diamonds. Don't forget that.For anyone reading this who is on a budget, but wants to buy something special for that certain someone, I suggest going to a reputable shop that sells used diamond jewelry. They are usually 1/2 the cost or less, but the quality is still there. I believe in "recycled" diamonds, because they don't ever lose their quality..and you can save A LOT of money. Normally those places only sell relatively top-quality anyway, so you can rest assured in getting a decent stone(s).Anyway, these are not as clear as the picture and each stone will vary. In other words, you may get one stone a little cloudy and the other not so much - so don't be surprised. These are not the great diamonds obviously, they are mediocre at best, but worth the price if it's not for someone you're trying to impress.Overall: Good price. Great size. Secure backs. Mediocre diamond with a lot of "debris", a little cloudy. Not brilliant. Good cut though, and a decent sparkle.I don't recommend for a gift, unless it's for someone who doesn't know the difference ;0)Hope this helps!

review: Between distance running and going through pregnancy, I've been buying more and more comfy "high-end" shoes this year (own a few Danskos). I'm all about comfort and style--as well as quality (I'm tough on my shoes).I had a pair of cheap (but cute) UGG knockoffs from Target that really didn't do much for the cold. So, when I saw a Gold Box deal featuring these EMUs for $40, I decided to give them a try. I already own a pair of the slippers--very toasty. I've never had actual UGGs, but I have to say these are pretty darn nice. They are *very* plush and warm inside and easy to walk in. Compared to my UGG knockoffs, they are actually quite a bit more flexible around the ankles. Like pretty much all of these styles of boots (including what I've read about UGGs), they don't offer arch support but the plush sheepskin does help a little.I run a women's 6.5-7 wide so I followed the advice other reviewers here and bought 7s. I was worried when I took the boot out of the box that they would be too big (they look very large and said they measured at a European 38), but they fit quite comfortably--snug (no slipping at all), but if I wanted to wear a couple pairs of socks I could still fit them on.They are very cute! They look nice with the cuff up or down and are very easy to fold down (I've had a lot of trouble doing that with my UGG knockoffs). The tread is very decent, too. I won't be ice fishing with them, but a lot of knockoffs I've seen have next to no tread so the 1/8" of logos on the bottom are a plus.I had hoped to buy the grey, but chestnut was all that was left in my size. It looks light on my monitor, but they appear darker in person (big plus as I'm not into light-colored boots). It's actually a good color to go with most winter outfits and will probably not pick up dirt too obviously.The quality is excellent. The stitching is *very* secure (no loose threads) though not too ornate (IMO, adds to the versatility of wearing them with different clothes). I see no defects on the outside or inside (wondering if the reviewer who complained about that got duds).I've tried them in the 20-40 degree weather (toasty but my feet don't get sweaty--also comfy indoors, too), but not snow yet. I'll be adding more later. :)

The majority of my Fitflop purchases have been from Amazon at great reduced prices! I am very thankful for Fitflop footwear because it allows me to be on my feet for extended periods of time w/ out resulting in a backache, and even on concrete! Thanks Fitflop! AND Amazon!

review: I've been dying for the J.Crew version, but was not about to drop a few hundred for it.  I am pretty thrilled with this piece.  I have only worn it twice, and I've gotten at least 3 dozen compliments.  And rightfully so; it's super cute!But let's be real.  This thing is $13. It's not real gold, the gems are resin mounted on plastic.  That said, the real thing would weigh about 15lbs.  This thing is comfy and light weight for the size.  I have quite a few pieces that have turned in the past, and here are some tips for keeping your jewelry looking its best:1.  Keep it in a cool, dry place.  If you have a humidifier in your bedroom, maybe put it somewhere else.  Don't leave it in the bathroom when you shower.2.  Don't spray anything directly on it.  That is body sprays, perfumes, spray deodorants, hair spray.  Let that stuff dry for 5-10 minutes before putting it on.3.  Don't let it get wet.  Think splashy hand washing, tooth brushing, sweating.  If you think you're going to get it wet or you're going to get hot, slip it under your collar or take it off and leave it somewhere dry.4.  If you do get it wet, pat it dry and then run it under a hair or hand dryer for a few seconds.I really like this piece, and sure, it's not top quality.  But with some care, I'm certain I can get the most of my $13.Other bonuses, love the Prime shipping.  Wish more of their products were.Five stars.  In love.

review: I have two pairs of this shoe - I bought my first pair from Macy's for $80, and a second pair from Amazon. That must mean I like the shoes, right? Well, read on...I thought by shelling out $80 I would get a decent pair that would last me awhile. I hate buying shoes and when I like a model, I buy it repeatedly. But after little more than a year, I was shocked to find that my May Poppy loafers (from Macy's) were no longer wearable...Like most shoes today, these May Poppies are made in sweatshop-friendly China and the sole is made of plastic, so don't expect these to last longer than a year or two. I'm glad I bought my second pair for a good price online - if I had to pay $100 or more (i.e., in a typical Clarks retail store) I would have felt ripped off.I didn't have sizing issues - my 8.5 M loafer fit perfectly. I wore my black May Poppy loafer every other day for work. I don't walk a lot for my job (typical desk-bound office worker) so the fact that they deteriorated after a year or so says something.The shoe is attractive and all, but beware the sole thins fast and you'll be left with an attractive shell but painful arches. I felt I was walking barefoot in the last months I kept this shoe. I had no choice but to throw them out even though, based on the deceptively nice-looking upper part, they still looked good.I've become resigned to the fact that Clarks will never be as good as it used to be (like every other company that's moved its business to China) and that my objective is never, ever to buy Clarks again at retail - it just isn't worth the money. I recommend trying them on at the shoe store, copying the model number and then coming here to Amazon for the much more reasonable prices. You'll be glad you did.
summary: Don't Pay Full Price - Soles Wear Out Fast, Feet Start to Hurt

review: I ordered these beautiful Fitflops for my Rick Steve's tour of Italy.  The tour involved a lot of walking. I have really bad feet; i.e. fallen arches, Plantar Fasciitis, and Chronic Venous Insuffiencency, which causes pain and swelling in my feet, ankles and legs.Pros:1. These stay on without scrunching my toes!  The design is such that the top comes up far enough for gravity to work on your side.  I had NO trouble keeping them on.2. Cushions every step.  I immediately felt the difference from the moment I put them on.  Very little to no impact upon my knees when walking great distances.  My knees never - ever hurt with these shoes.3. Beautiful - I have received several compliments on the style.  They are quite fashionable and go well with dresses, skirts, slacks, jeans and shorts.4. No plantar fasciitis pain - at all. This was so wonderful that I had to pinch myself. I have owned, Merrill, Born, and Clarks and these simple little things out-shined them all combined.5.Unfortunately, my feet and ankles did swell excessively during the trip, but the sandals always fit!  The design allowed for the top of my feet to swell without adding any pressure or making it hard to put them on.  My ankles were the same way. Even with feet and ankles the size of footballs, they were STILL comfortable.It is not as though I had no pain during the heavy walking days of the tour, but anyone would have no matter what they put on their feet.  I am a photographer and I normally take an extra pair of shoes with my on event shoots because my feet start to kill me.  I did a 3 hour shoot and never thought of my feet once!  Fabulous!Cons:1. I have average width feet and these are slender and attractive in the toe area compared to the athletic styles. The downside is that they are just barely too narrow in the big toe area.  After wearing them for two solid weeks, I developed small blisters and large calluses on the sides of my big toes.2. These are not fit for all terrain use.  In Florence the streets are old cobblestone and very uneven.  The height of these and lack of ankle straps combined with the cobblestone equaled a fall when I hit uneven patches with both feet.I've been wearing these exclusively!  You would have to pry them out of my cold dead hands to get them away from me.

review: Love me my automatic watches.  However, frilly Swiss watches that cost a small fortune -- though certainly fine timepieces, were never my style. Give me a good ole Japanese Seiko or Orient (another excellent Japanese mechanical watchmaker) automatic any day, and I'm happy. This watch is a basic steel silver dial watch in the Seiko 5 style.  It is powered by their infamous 7S26B watch movement. Unlike more complicated automatic watch movements, the 7S26 is notorious for its simplicity, durability, and longevity.  The watchmaker I took it too to have a link removed was familiar - said this will keep time for 20 years without need of having the movement cleaned/re-oiled (the reason most mechanical watches stop working), as is their reputation.  One of the ways Seiko kept the cost very reasonable was by making the steel band hollow - a concession I'm willing to make.  The band seems sturdy enough, fits like a glove after having a link removed, and is light and comfortable.  The dial is no frills and icy silver - just what I wanted. It is very easy to set the day and date, as is the case with the entire Seiko 5 line.  The size of the dial is small by modern standards (and my other watches). That will take a little getting used to, would prefer it a tad bigger. The "5" in Seiko 5 means: 1. Diaflex (unbreakable)  mainspring, 2. Diashock (shock resistant) design, 3. automatic movement, 4. day/date indicator, and 5. water resistant. Bottom line - unassuming, very affordable, durable, accurate, yet elegant automatic timepiece for everyday wear that - if well cared for and with some luck, may provide decades of service and enjoyment.
summary: Hard to Beat a Seiko 5 Automatic - Timeless

review: Wow! I love this bag.  I have been on a quest for the perfect bag and may have just found it. They also make it unlikely the bag will accidentally come open allowing items to fall out.This bag also holds a tremendous amount comfortably for its small size.Currently, I can COMFORTABLY carry the following, with no bag distortion:small calendarIpad, in bulky leather protective caseLARGE wallet (contains checkbook, about 20 credit/id cards, several individually wrapped Tazo tea bags, about half a dozen splenda packs, cash, a couple of dental floss picks)Case Logic padded pouch (contains video ipod, earbuds, electric wall charging adaptor and two cords to charge ipad/ipod and phone, jump drive with neck strap4+ pens/pencilsseveral kinds of meds. 3 of these each take about the space of an inhaler. I also keep a small weekly med organizer in here. The smaller items are in a cloth pouch.CarmexTube of abrevatwo bottles of 5-hour energyKey-bak Retractable Key Reel with 48 inch Kevlar Cord -- complete with multiple keys for work.Second key ring for house/car keys.Hairbrush2-4 protein bars or similar snacksOuter pockets can hold 2 bottles of water OR 1 bottle of water and a compact umbrella. If not carrying an umbrellaCrushable shopping bag or two -- the type you can use to hold purchase while out and about for the day.There is leftover space. I can easily add tickets and small paper items to the back pocket where I store a printout of my calendar for quick reference.There is also space where I could add the following items while traveling on a plane (I would remove them after arriving at my destination when I would use this as a daypack/purseSmall bag of extra snacks in case of air delaysSleep maskInflatable pillowIf I wanted to travel with ONLY this as a carry-on, I "could" manage to fit the following in without too much discomfort, as well:ziplock bag of cosmetics (foundation, coverup, eye liner, mascara, blush, powder, lip gloss).Spare ziplock bag or twoLightweight crushable tshirt, undergarments, and socks in case of lost luggage.Microfiber washcloth with ziplock bag -- nice for washing up in the airport or on the plane.Realize that adding all of this would not leave much extra space in the bag!!!!

review: Before I give a review of the dress I just want to pass along some important information.  I keep seeing women (adults) who keep buying dresses from Pacificplex and complaining that the dresses do not fit.  It's always the same... "They are too small!"  Please, dear reader, look at the info... this is a JR. SIZE!  Pacificplex makes clothes for JR. SIZES.  That means it is for young girls.  They are cut smaller, made smaller, and are made for young girls in mind.  Jr. Sizes clothing is as much as 2 sometimes 3 sizes smaller than the regular misses sizes you see on-line or in the store.  IF you are an adult woman, like me, who likes clothes that don't look like they were made w/ a 90 year old in mind, do yourself a favor... USE A TAPE MEASURE!  Do NOT be vain and say, "I can't be a 2x!"  If you are willing to measure yourself, not be vain, and actually read about the sizes you will be more than pleased w/ the sizing!  I am a bigger busted, NEARLY plus sized, adult woman, and I have ordered from Pacificplex many times and the dresses ALWAYS fit.  A friendly bit of advice... use a tape measure! ;-)As for this dress... I LOVE IT!  It's so cute and fits well!  It falls right to the middle of my knee (I am 5' 2.5") and is actually quite slimming.  One note though... there is plenty of room in the top!  I am rather busty (38 DD) and it fit me w/ room to spare.  I didn't try it on w/ a bra so it might be tighter when I do, but I think for a normal busted woman there would be plenty of room!  In a lot of reviews they mention that the dress isn't taffeta.  While this dress isn't the "common/true" taffeta, it is still a type of taffeta even if it is thin.  It has the rustle of taffeta and is not so shiny that it looks like satin.  I plan on wearing this on a cruise for a semi-formal night and I feel this dress will be perfect!  The color is true ivory and even comes w/ a matching long scarf that you can throw over your shoulders (I plan on doing this!) if it gets chilly or you don't want to leave your back open.  I am sad to see that other colors are sold out in my size.  This dress fits the bill if you want something light, fun, and a little sassy.  Pacificplex is THE BEST!
summary: Wonderful Dress... Use a Tape Measure!

review: I purchased the Casio Men's G-Shock Atomic Tough Solar Watch for three main reasons:1) I wanted an inexpensive yet rugged watch to wear on weekends or when hiking, etc.2) I love having the EXACT time. This clock automatically sets itself to the exact time using radio transmissions from the U.S. Atomic Clock in Colorado. This does not require any sort of subscription - it's free and is an automatic process.3) Solar-powered! No more batteries to change. The battery can last up to eight months on a full charge without further exposure to light.Why I love this watch:I had a G-Shock in the 1980s when I was in college. It was a great watch and was quite trendy at the time. And it was also indestructible and lasted for several years.That's why I love the "retro-look" of the Casio Men's G-Shock Atomic Tough Solar Watch. It brings back some great memories of my old G-Shock -- it looks and feels like an old friend on my wrist.Again, I love having the "exact" time. I'm frustrated by having several clocks around the house (wall clocks, bedside clocks, my windup wristwatch, clocks on the VCR, my car clock, coffeemaker, etc.) all with slightly different times. I now use my G-Shock to set all of these clocks to the exact time. And that feels good.I love this watch and look forward to having it for several years to come! :-)Hope this helps!

review: I openly admit I am a HUGE fan of distressed clothing. I have just never liked the brand new look especially with T-shirts, sweatshirts, blue jeans, and chino's. I am not into the heavily distressed look with big rips and holes everywhere but a nice subtle distress job can turn a great piece of clothing into one of my favorites. With that being said distressed clothing can be expensive. Its ironic as your paying more, sometimes considerably more, for clothing that is basically used and is not going to have as long of a lifespan. That is why I decided to learn how to distress my own clothing. I have been doing it for years now and have become very good at it. Its actually not as hard as some might think and not only does it save you money but it also allows you to distress an item exactly how you like it and exactly where you want it.I often get asked by some of my family members to distress a particular piece of clothing and that is where Carhartt comes into play as my nephew loves Carhartt and he has had me distress a couple of these jackets over the last year. The reason I am bringing this up is because I have learned that jackets like these are actually perfect for distressing. In fact I ended up liking the job I did on this particular jacket so much that I would up hooking myself up with one. The shell material, which is 100% cotton, is very similar to that of blue jeans which is why it lends itself so well to distressing. To be honest I actually didn't even care much for this jacket when it was brand new. Dont get me wrong its a nice jacket, it just goes back to the whole I don't like new sweatshirts or blue jeans problem I have, lol. Once I had given this jacket a nice round of distressing it just really changed the entire look of the jacket. I have actually had multiple compliments on the jacket since I started wearing it, in fact I even had someone ask me if I distressed it myself and then offer to pay me if I would do one from them. I politely declined as its actually quite a time consuming process and I just don't have that sort of time right now. Its was still nice to hear.Overall this is a really nice jacket that is actually a lot warmer than I though it was going to be. While I don't particularly like the "New" look of the jacket off the rack, a couple rounds of distressing quickly turned it into one of my favorites. If you know how to distress clothing for yourself and you like this jacket then you might want to consider giving it a go. Again this style jacket is absolutely perfect for distressing. In fact I just ordered a Carhartt Sandstone ridge jacket a couple days back and plan on doing the same thing to that jacket.Off the rack - 3 starsDistressed - 5 stars!!

review: I've been using rubber strap-ons, or better, streeeeetch-ons, for years.  They worked fine as long as I didn't try to climb over icy snow mounds or hike through the woods since, if I got stuck on a root or hung up on hidden ice chunks, I'd suddenly find myself without the cleats.  Finding the missing one was one thing, but getting it back on without falling over (there's never any tree to lean against when needed) was a different issue all together.  Plus they tended to slide around on the boot and I'd end up walking on only half a row.So I decided to give these Stabilizers a try although I was a bit hesitant despite the great reviews.  Reason:  I was worried that snow would pile in between boot and Stabilizer and make for heavy, possibly sloshy walking.  Not so at all!I had to undergo a complete knee replacement revision just two months ago, my second knee replacement surgery in 15 months, so needless to say, I have to be careful not to suddenly twist the operated leg since a fracture could spell disaster.  But I'm an outdoor person, not a couch potato, and I love going out in ice and snow and hike with our dog and hope to even snowshoe again this winter.  Right now, however, with the first storms bearing down on us in the Northeast, and lots of ice and crusty snow on the ground out in the woods and meadows, these Stabilizers are a God-send.  Yesterday I even ran a little here and there with them on uneven ice, that's how well they perform (just don't tell my surgeon...).  I would NOT have attempted that with the old stretch-ons, but these things stay put and make me feel safe as can be.  These have no carbide points; rather, they are solid steel round screwlike studs with a hollow center, and the outer, grooved ring of each stud bites into ice securely.  I used to slip a tad with the carbide points, but not once, not even a tiny bit, with these.Great product, velcro fasteners are easy to use, highly recommend for anyone who needs or wants to be out when it's icy.

review: I own two of these Libian shrugs. This one in black and the Libian Plus Size 3/4 Zebra Animal Print Bolero Cardigan. I was told that these shrugs run small. These are JUNIOR Plus sizes, not WOMENS plus sizes. In a WOMENS Plus size, a 1X would be just right or a bit big on me, depending on the cut and the garment. In these JUNIOR Plus shrugs, it was suggested I order a 3X.Oddly, though both of these shrugs are marked on Amazon as JUNIOR plus, and I ordered a 3X in both, they fit me completely differently. This black shrug fits snugly through the shoulders and sleeves, but the front panels barely come to the side/middle of my bust on each side. Additionally, this shrug is SUPER short from the collar to the bottom hem and the front panels are almost nonexistent, so there is zero chest/tummy coverage on this one. The zebra print one comes clear across my bust (though not all the way closed from top to bottom) and, while the center back is not much longer than this one, the front panels are longer and better shaped to cover more territory in the chest/midriff area.So it all depends on what you want. If all you care about is having your arms covered, this shrug will be great for you. If, like me, you would prefer something with a bit more coverage, then you may like the zebra print one better. I don't think that going up in size would help me much on this shrug, because there is so little fabric in the front.Regarding fabric construction, this black shrug's tag lists the following:46% rayon46% polyester8% spandexI was a bit surprised (and, truthfully, disappointed) to find that the washing instructions indicate only hand washing. I thought with something that had that much polyester, it could be machine washed. I guess not because of the rayon.I'd say this shrug is in moderate territory when it comes to workmanship. It's not the best-made garment I've ever seen, but it is also not the worst. My shrug had matched and well-sealed seams and no threads hanging off of it.This shrug feels more sweater-like than the zebra one, which is what I was expecting. It is very lightweight sweater material, though. The fabric is very soft. Something great for a cool spring or summer evening more than for the dead of winter.For ME, this shrug merits a 3 1/2. For someone smaller/younger, who just wants arm coverage, it may well be a 4 or 5. The manufacturer offered this product to me at a reduced price in exchange for a review.
summary: I like the lightweight sweater-like fabric but wish it had more coverage.

review: It really is incredibly flattering. It hits that sweet spot where it's snug enough to show off your shape, but thick enough material not to show every little bump. I would call it conservatively sexy- tight enough to wear to a dinner date without feeling frumpy, but loose enough to wear to work without being ostentatious. I am 5'8" and 140-145, and the 9/10 was perfect. Two notes: one of the small string beltloops arrives broken (I asked for a replacement, and the same one was broken, so I think they come out of the factory this way) so be prepared to cut them off and have the belt be unsecured. Second, the back zips up very, very high. I managed to get it up on my own, but it was really tough and I kind of have monkey arms. If you have any dexterity problems or are shy about asking someone to zip you up, you might have trouble with this. There is a small hole in the zipper though, so you might be able to feed some dental floss or a thin ribbon through it and hold both ends and pull.

review: I bought this watch in early 2004 and it is just as solid and beautiful as the day I bought it over ten years ago. Not like ten years is some enormous amount of time, but any big watch guy knows that's quite a while to hang on to a timepiece. At least for me. I tend to flip watches with regularity and I'm really glad I have hung on to this one. I ordered it via Amazon when I was at Fort Huachuca, AZ for Army training and I wore it in Iraq for a 12-month tour. After that I wore it less often, but not because of any specific issue, I just moved on to the newest and coolest watch. Anyway, I recently rediscovered this Seiko masterpiece in my case and I am really glad I have it still. Unlike the current issue model, mine has a Seiko Singapore movement, rather than a Malaysian movement. But in both cases, my understanding is that the watch is assembled in Japan using the same movement. So the precise origin of the movement's construction may not be a big deal. Anyway, mine keeps great time, given its cost. Obviously, it can't keep pace with a Casio Solar Atomic model, which is another favorite of mine, but it still is pretty accurate. I've worn mine all day for many days now and its accuracy is about +5 to 7 seconds per 24 hours. I think that's pretty good. And whatever concern you may have over the inherent inaccuracy of Seiko automatics versus general quartz movements, I will say that the absolute aesthetic beauty of this watch greatly outweighs them. It is a stunner. From the robust case width and bold color scheme to the jubilee band and fit and finish, this model is a clear win. I highly recommend it to anyone seeking a classic retro diver look.
summary: Solid as a rock, over a decade later. Excellent watch.

The more I checked them out, put checks/registers in and out and got a feel for them - the less I liked them. Just felt "cheap" and I knew they would wear out very quickly. If you are into religious or inspirational sayings on your products- this line will be tops for you. I didn't really need that in my product, was more concerned with price/color/quality, but am pleased with the entire product now - sayings included. review: I have now purchased 3 pair of these jeans and they have all had the same fit. I did not notice any difference between them. I have bought the dark stonewashed, black and silver fox. The silver fox was a thinner and softer type of material and I liked it a lot! The best thing about ordering your jeans through amazon is that you can always find your size. I have always had a hard time finding 36x36 in the 505's. It's minimally more expensive but at least I know they will have what I need. No more frustration of hunting through the stack of jeans at a store hoping that someone put your size in the wrong bin!4 stars because these jeans are still no where near the same quality of the jeans I got in the early 80's when they were made in america. I hold onto them hoping I will eventually fit back into my 34 waist. If you have any original levi's before 1985 I would hold onto them. They are in great demand. They have everything going for them. They are high quality original Made in America blue jeans and are now retro. win-win. if you have ANY levi's from the 50's and 60's they can easily command $500 or more depending on the condition. Children sizes are the most in demand because they were well worn and sell the best, so they are the hardest to find.*update* 8-7-2012My fourth pair was not sewn right. It was a pair of the Dark Stonewash and the seam on the right leg would rotate to the top of my knee when sitting. review: Don't really expect much from a belt. No one sees them and they're really just to keep my pants covering all the goodies. This belt has changed my perspective. The manufacturer has engineered a masterpiece. The leather comes in a customizable length, simply cut it to fit. On the black leather, the waist lengths are marked, but should be cut a quarter of an inch more as you have to account for the portion that the teeth must pinch down so that the buckle holds in place. On the white leather, the lengths are not marked (or maybe they are, but marked in white which defeats the purpose). I used my previously purchased black belt for measuring. The stitching and the inset teeth for the buckle to hold are both well produced and very sturdy. The buckle is beautiful and the release latch is simple. You can't tell it's the latch as it fits flush with the rest of the buckle. The only thing I fear is scratching the buckle. The belt is nice enough for a shirt and tie, possibly suit depending on your taste, and definitely everyday wear. It's a dynamic belt and couldn't recommend it more.
summary: A new staple in the wardrobe

review: This bra surpasses every other bra I have tried for comfort and fit! For a large breasted lady, it is hard to find a wire-free bra with support that is also comfortable. This bra has it all! It is a shaping bra with light padding, with the comfort of a sport bra - but without the uni-breast look of a sports bra. It seems to have been designed on a sports bra concept but with a clip back for adjustments. The cups separate and support, and the wide band along the bottom gives enough support without the discomfort of other bras I have tried lately - no itching or digging. The shoulder straps are not adjustable but are stretchy enough to adapt to your body. At last I can live without the constant distractions of an uncomfortable bra! Comfort as well as good fit  - simple sizing - and great price -finally a bra manufacturer figured it out!

review: I was so glad to have these boots when we were hit with 15" of snow. They are pretty easy to get on because of the slightly stretchy top and shoe neck and are warmer than you'd think.  I had to shovel my way to the barn and my feet never felt cold, but even after wearing them inside for a while, thinking I might have to go back out again, my feet didn't get hot either which surprised me.  It didn't seem like they'd breathe but apparently they do somehow.  They slid off very easily with a bootjack.For sizing, they run a little big which gives you room for warm socks.  Both foot and top are lined with smooth cushioned fabric which seems to hug your foot so they feel especially comfortable inside, not just a rubber shell.I've had the boots for a while but never had occasion to wear them before this big snow.  They really served me well.  My big toes have gone rigid due to arthritis so normally I can't get tall boots on, but these have enough give to let me slide in with a lot less effort than I expected.  I also have wide calves, but the tops have enough give to fit nicely while not binding.  I wish they had even more foot support than they do, but the support is decent and gave me enough flexibility to adapt to the uneven footing in the packed snow and I felt very secure.  After a couple of days of melting and refreezing, I added YakTrax Yaktrax Walker Traction Cleats for Snow and Ice to the bottoms to have stable footing on the ice as well.  Because of the rim around the sole of the boot, the Yaktrax stayed securely on and I could do what I needed to without fear of falling.All in all these boots are great in the snow and highly recommended.

review: I've bought the Maidenform One Fabulous Fit bra for the past five or six years. I love these bras because they are comfortable, they provide good support, and they don't tend to create any lines under clothes. Good basic bras.However, in the past year or so I've noticed a change in the sizing. Ever since I started buying these, I've been a 38B. Last year, though, I bought two of these in 38B to replace two others that I'd worn out, and the new bras felt waaay too small -- they cut in around my ribs and across my chest (which created a  wildly unflattering, spilling-over silhouette under my clothes! That had never happened with my old ones). To be fair, bras tend to fit snugly when you first buy them, but when I compared the new ones with the older ones, the cups on the new ones were a lot smaller: they did not come up as high across the front, and they were narrower across the bottom. I wonder if Maidenform switched manufacturers or something, because until then I'd always found the sizing consistent for this model, even across different colors. I decided to go up a size and see if that helped, and it did: the cups on the new 38C are about the same size as those on an old 38B (perhaps a hair larger across the bottom, but they give me the right coverage on top).That said, I do still recommend this bra because it is comfy and well-made, but I'd suggest going up a cup size over your normal size to get a good fit!
summary: Good bras, but the sizing has changed in the past few years

review: Of course, shoes are very subjective. All of our feet are so different. So to make a shoe to fit all and feel comfortable to all is really impossible. But if you wear rubber sandals aka flip flops, you should consider the Okabashi brand. They make a great assortment of sandals.I'm from Florida. I was born a beach boy. So from the youngest age, I have worn flip flops. From a teen on up my feet have been a mens 11. A large foot. So finding flip flops to fit was never easy.Working in the Bahamas, I picked up a pair of Reefs that I dearly loved and wore them for ten years, until one day, they came apart. Ugh. I have always hated shopping and looking for replacements, I found out that the Reef brand, where once a top quality brand, was no longer at that level. Broke my heart. So the search began for a top quality, inexpensive replacement for my beloved Reefs.I quickly came upon Okabashi. Now I almost passed them by because of the name. I wanted to buy American made and that is obviously a foreign name. But in reading I found out that the company is named after the designer and that all of their products are made in the USA! I also found out that they are sold in a lot of local stores. So off to CVS to try on a pair. I couldn't believe how soft the rubber felt, so much softer than the Reefs. The toe post is thick, but my feet are large so that wasn't a problem. It may be for you if you have slender feet with close toes.But the thing that will sell you OR be a deal breaker is the raised instep with "massage beads". At first, these beads felt strange to the bottoms of my instep. But in a very short period of time, I not only got used to them, I can't wear any other sandal! They are wonderful on your feet and those beads really do sooth your feet as you walk. Plus, the raised sole has continued to keep me free of plantar fasciitis, which I suffered from for years! And you can wash these on the top shelf of your dishwasher!Not only are they worth the retail price, CVS runs deep sales at the end of each summer, up to 75% off the list price!So if you are looking for American made, and need some rubber sandals, do check these out if you get the chance!PROS:Relatively InexpensiveSoft RubberRaised InstepSoft Massage Beads On InstepVarious Sizes And StylesTwo Year No Questions Asked Exchange WarrantyMade In USAVery ComfortableDishwasher SafeCONS:Heavy On Small FeetThick Toe PostMassage Beads And Raised Instep May Feel Odd To SomeOne more thing:Cheap flip flops can damage your feet, causing many painful problems. And cheap flip flops can't be washed in the extremely hot water of the dishwasher which kills the 30,000 plus types of bacteria that can grow on flip flops. I wash mine with alcohol and anti-bacterial soap constantly. So be mindful that you may find cheap flip flops in the corner store, but if you wear them a lot, you may be causing lots of problems for your feet. Just do an online search for "flip flop foot problems" and educate yourself so you'll have happy feet!
summary: I've Worn Flip Flops For 50 Years And These Are The Best!

review: I have a pair of 505 in the same size as the new pair I ordered on Amazon.  The previous pair was bought on sale at a local Fred Meyer.  They were durable and fit well.  They actually lasted over a year with frequent wearing and once per week washing (I estimate around 40-50 washes).  So I bought the same size and fit on Amazon just in a darker color to wear at work.  I also bought a pair of the 515 slim fit Levi's a few weeks ago and they were perfect and fit exactly the same as another pair I own.  But I wanted the less 'slim' fit of the 505s for work, also wanted the regular and not low rise waist.  So I ordered the same exact size and style:Bad idea!The 505s I got came with a tag saying 'sits below waist', which is NOT what they used to say.  Before, the 505s were a zipper fly version of the 501s.  Putting the pair of 505s I already owned next to the new pair from Amazon, I could see the crotch to waist was almost an inch shorter on the new pair.  Trying the new pair on, I discovered a most unpleasant sensation of my 'man parts' being crushed in agonizing ways.  No amount of tugging would get the pants to sit right.Then I tried on the 515 slim jeans, and they were actually ROOMIER in the crotch area.  The thighs were a little slimmer than the 505s, but the seat was the main difference.  No sag in the seat of the 515s compared to the 505.And then in trying on the recently purchased 515s (in the same color as the new 505s I purchased) I noticed something interesting.  The denim in the new pair was thinner and tougher feeling than in the 515s.  It wasn't just the feeling of the jeans prior to washing.  The 515s never felt that tough, and the denim was thicker in the side by side comparison.I returned them based on a misleading product description (no mention of low rise waist) and inferior quality.  I gave the 515s a 5 star rating because they were as good as the previous 515s that I bought at a high price from a department store.  The previous 505s were also great (Fred Meyer of all places - though they don't have the 505s anymore... booo).  So I'm sticking with the 515s until something changes with them.  I'll take the slightly low waist and save my manhood.  And the better quality denim means they'll last longer anyway.  So here are my comparisons:Levi's 505 purchased 9/10/12 from Amazon - 1 starLevi's 505 purchased fall 2011 from Fred Meyer - 5 starLevi's 515 purchased 6/22/2012 from Amazon - 5 starJust be aware that the product might differ slightly from what is described, and that the quality appears to vary from order to order.  I suspect that is why the great divide in 1 and 5 star reviews.  I love Levi's jeans, and I will continue to buy them, but I will be a little more careful with the online purchasing.  And remember, if you buy from Amazon, they will return or exchange any unwashed clothing items.
summary: Not the same as they once were ~

review: You know it's just too bad what has happened to this country concerning business and manufacturing. Greed has pushed much of our manufacturing overseas and we just keep buying the now inferior products and don't do much about it. So sad.Walmart years ago used to sell only Made In USA products but then fell to greed. NBC Dateline did a story on how Walmart closed down a sweater manufacturing plant in North Carolina and moved the plant to Bangladesh. Their reasoning in putting over 100 Americans out of work and going overseas was that they could save 10 cents a sweater in doing so. Ten cents! Folks over here would have gladly paid ten cents MORE a sweater to have USA made goods and to keep our fellow Americans in work. But alas this is now the norm. I have to say shame on Hanes for doing this. But off of my soapbox...for now.I bought a pair of these Hanes sweat pants many years ago and they were absolutely the best investment for the money! They fit perfectly, were soft and comfortable, inexpensive, and lasted me 18 years of pretty heavy use. Made in the USA. So it was time to replace them and I wanted the same thing. I should have known better. Ugh!I found the exact same model Hanes sweat pants here on Amazon and put them in my Birthday wish list. I received them shortly after. Here's what I got.I got the exact same model. They are indeed soft. But I've only worn them a few times and they've only been washed twice. So we'll see how long they last.I got the exact same size as listed in the old pair of Hanes sweat pants: Adult Large (36-38). So why do they change things? I'm swimming in these things! the inseam is the same. They don't bunch at the foot so the length is okay but the elastic at the ankles doesn't begin to cinch up enough. The waist is okay as the elastic gives enough depending on how large I happen to be any particular week! But the legs are HUGE! The originals were just right. Why add so much material that my wife could jump in them with me and we'd still have too much space? I know I only use them to lounge around the house when it's cold and when I take power walks. But still, they are SO large you'd think I had massively huge legs!I know, that's not really a downside considering Americans today are in such poor shape and are so super-sized themselves. But I'd just love it one time that I'd replace something with the same thing and it was the same as it used to be or better. Is that asking too much of American companies? Where is your quality control?All of this just makes me not have any brand loyalty anymore. I will from now on shop not by brand traditions, or just price, but quality will take on a much higher level of importance than in the past. Not good for long time companies like Hanes. And I will be checking out the various "Made In USA" websites that have appeared over recent years. Yes, I'll pay more for quality and for keeping Americans (legal Americans) working. But hopefully I'll also get better made products.Rant mode off!UPDATE January 2014:With the cooler weather I've been wearing these pants as my indoor lounging pants and take my outdoor power walks in them (which works okay in Florida during the winter). But I've dropped the rating another star. The pants are coming apart. Even after several washings, there are large black tufts of the pant material all over the house. However these were made, they still "feel" soft but the pants are just coming apart. It looks like we have some kind of animal in the house who is shedding! I wore the original Hanes pants for twenty years and they never ever did this. So be fore warned!
summary: Like Almost Everything Today...Not The Quality They Used To Be

review: Bought these mostly to wear while walking our big, quick-paced, powerful dog on the icy roads.  They work really well!  Our footing is sure and secure and we don't worry about slipping anymore!  These are somewhat easy to put on; they do require a few firm tugs, but even with arthritis, we are able to manage this.  (Eventually, we just used our old pair of boots for these and left them on.  It was quicker and easier each day.)  They are fairly comfortable to wear; you know they are on your shoes, but we can still walk comfortably and pretty quick-paced.  They are lighter than most ice cleats are on your shoes.  They stay securely on your feet while walking.  As for sizing, the sizes stated fit most shoes and slimmer boots, but if you wear bulkier boots, such as Sorels or Kamiks (or are in the upper limit of the size range), you will need/be more comfortable in the next larger size.  These seem very well-made; as for durability, we walk several miles per day and after about a week, we've noticed considerable wear on the diamond spikes, but, so far, they still work.  We don't think they will last more than a winter, if that, but even so, we have bought another pair and can't imagine walking on the slippery snow and ice without them.  Good product; best we have found/used so far.  Would like to see more durable spikes, but will definitely buy again!  UPDATE:  Well, I have to lower my rating a bit, from 4 to 3 stars.  Depending on road conditions, such as more or less bare spots versus ice/snow coverage, a pair of these lasted about 3-5 weeks, on average.  We did not always wear them daily, especially later in the winter.  One would break, then the other soon after.  We were able to fix many of them to extend their usefulness by piecing the good parts together.  It was always the wire holding the beads that broke, never the rubber strap.  One other thing to note was that the rubber strap size would vary a bit from pair to pair, so when we bought a new pair, they did not always fit the same.  This wasn't a big deal, but was mildly annoying, as it felt different, as though we had the "wrong" size.  We went through 6 pair collectively and just made it through the winter.  More expensive than we had planned, but better than getting hurt.  Still a better product than anything else we've tried, but will keep looking for something cheaper/better!

review: This is most likely the most supportive bra I've ever worn. I'm almost 60 so you can bet I've tried just about every size and style out there and always seemed to choose an underwire style. However, having just completed 3 years of cancer treatments/remissions/relapses with illness causing me to lose weight, meds causing me to gain weight, the sedentary life style from being so ill as well as the infamous menopausal 50 lbs along for the ride I was feeling like an unfit, sloppy blob. I had pretty much been wearing cheap sports bras (you know, the 3 pack for about $9 kind) simply for the comfort and ease in dressing/undressing at the hospital and doctors' offices. Finally I got fed up with feeling lousy and looking lazy and sloppy and did some comparative shopping. Ordered several bra styles in several sizes and had myself a 'trying on party' when they all arrived. This bra is my favorite right now. The gel straps are amazingly comfortable and don't dig into my shoulders, pull on my neck or fall down the sides of my arms. My 'girls' look perkier than they have in about 20 years and no longer slouch against my midsection (wow, did I just really admit to that?), and the criss cross elastic straps in the back somehow, without discomfort, make me stand up straight and improve my posture tremendously.Now all that above is the good stuff. What I'm finding now is that at the bottom of the front closure where the two sides meet, it digs into the area at the base of my sternum and in just a few hours has caused a gouge in my skin: almost looks like a bad heel blister from new shoes. A kind of scratchy, digging, wish-it-would-stop sensation and I find myself picking at the base of the bra closure through my shirt and adjusting it more and more as the day wears on. I'm not sure if it will soften up when laundered but I hope so. Otherwise I'll have to find something like a cotton ball to stick under there if I want to wear this bra for any length of time. I have no idea what would happen if I was more active: perhaps I would have noticed this irritation sooner, but I'm not allowed to leave the house for any reason due to my compromised immune system and flu season, so I'm not doing much other than housework and playing with the dogs. So right now I can say this is a great bra for fit and support but the front closure edges could use a bit more padding.Edit: May 2012 - meant to write this much sooner but I forgot as I stopped wearing these and put them in the bottom of the drawer. Laundering does not soften up the lower portion of the closure. It remains hard and stiff and irritating to the sternum area. As much as these fit extremely well, the straps were a delight and my posture was vastly improved, I simply could not wear these without tucking a cotton ball or balled up toilet paper into that bottom section of the hook/closure area. They are going into the 'donate' pile today.

It's a very versatile winter layer, and a little more stylish than your basic turtleneck.Problems are: The flat seams pucker a little and are scratchy. The fabric itself isn't as soft as I hoped for something to wear next to my skin under sweaters. The upper arms are tight, but I have that problem with most shirts.

review: This is my second pair of New Balance shoes. The first pair is from about ten years ago. I still wear them; they are comfortable but getting scruffy looking. I bought that pair when my foot doctor recommended the New Balance brand for pain in my arches (I usually have to wear a drug store variety arch support). He was right. They helped.That's why I looked hard for my next pair and these came with good recommendations from other reviewers. They were right about one thing: consider ordering, as I did, one half size or more larger than your usual size. For me a size 9 wide is a minimum size for comfort. I ordered these in mens size 9 1/2 EE (wide) and if I had tried them on without knowing the size I would have thought they were a size 9EE.So, for me, going up 1/2 size (I typically order a European size 43 or a 9.5M nowadays) yielded about the right size. Can't predict your experience. Wish shoe sizes ordered online were more predictable, but that's why there are reviews.You can look over the user photos I uploaded to see these as worn with my heavy hunting socks. I'll keep these. They are OK though I prefer slightly more room at the toe.The Good:Price - hardly US $75,Materials - handsome leather, non-marking sole, extensive interior lining, round laces that hold well,Design - not gaudy color but a tasteful combination of tanned dark leather and lighter (taupe) that might serve for 'casual Friday' at the office,  or mall walking, light trail hiking, or for all those people who are on their feet most of the day and need a comfortable shoe. This might be the one.The Bad:Not much - just watch the sizing and try to make an intelligent guess based on these reviews. Don't wear it outside of the house (so you can return it to Amazon) until you are sure the sizing is right. After walking a couple of miles in these New Balance Mens MW967 Country Walking Shoes I find them to be tight in the toe i.e., too short in length even though I ordered a half size larger than usual. So, perhaps a full size larger than usual size would be best as an ordering strategy. This is not the way to run a shoe store. I may stop buying shoes on Amazon.

review: I have always admired the Baby Lupah Invicta watches because they looked very pretty and elegant.  I love the silver tilt of the softly rounded watch glass. Unfortunately, it is one of those pretty but useless touches.  When you see a picture of it, the numbers are very clear; however while in use, the watch always has to be tilted one way or another to see the numbers.  This watch is good if you only want it to be pretty and you're going to look at your phone for the time anyway.It's not that big of a problem to tilt the watch to see the time since the watch is always traveling and you already have to bring it around to the front to see the watch face anyway.  That's the really annoying part about it.  Most of the time I am wearing it, the actual watch face is on the outside of my wrist, where it is most likely to get banged up.  On the other hand, so to speak, no amount of banging has caused the watch to get scratched or to go off time.I have been wearing this watch since the day it arrived trying to make it be more flexible.  It has been everywhere during the past two months even to a 7-year-old's laser tag birthday party where it was covered with chocolate frosting and cake.  It has never become softer and more flexible.  The inside of the band is sort of sueded so it is more comfortable.  But then it has the ring of scratchy and annoying leather to catch the end of the band.Because watch bands have never fit me, I have tended to like big dangly watches or stretchy bracelet watches.  My wrist is very small and bands are loose.  To put a new hole in it is to make it fit so closely that it cuts off circulation.  I have never been able to find the right in-between notch that will allow it to stay face up on my wrist and not cut off circulation or to be comfortably loose but not floating all over the place.Of all of the Invicta watches I have tried, this one is the most problematic.  Well, actually, it's the only problematic one.
